Centralize resolution so agent + human paths share one source of truth.

title: STT broken: AudioBridge passes pre-0.4.2 args to mlx_audio.stt.generate (3 MCP tools fail)
description: memos_transcribe, memos_summarize, and memos_capture_to_reminders all fail under mlx-audio 0.4.2 (current installed version; doctor reports it 'available'). AudioBridge.transcribe (pippin/AudioBridge/AudioBridge.swift:160-164) builds: [filePath, --model, model, --format, outputFormat]. mlx-audio 0.4.2's stt.generate requires: --audio <file> (NOT positional), a REQUIRED --output-path <path>, and --format in {txt,srt,vtt,json} (pippin passes 'text', which 0.4.2 rejects). 0.4.2 also writes the transcript to --output-path rather than stdout, so the stdout-JSON parse at lines 172-186 must be reworked to read the output file. PROOF: invoking mlx_audio.stt.generate --model mlx-community/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2 --audio <m4a> --output-path /tmp/x --format txt transcribes a real Voice Memo perfectly; pippin's positional form is rejected at the argparse usage line. doctor's mlx-audio check only probes import+version, never the call signature, so it stays green. Default model alias 'parakeet' may also need mapping to a full HF id. Fix: update arg construction for 0.4.2 (probably gate on detected mlx_audio version since the binary-discovery layer already distinguishes 0.4.2+ vs legacy stt). Add an integration test that actually shells out to a transcription. Found during full 44-tool MCP E2E (41/44 pass; these 3 are the only failures).
close_reason: Fixed: AudioBridge now builds the mlx-audio 0.4.2 stt.generate arg vector (--audio/--output-path/--format json), maps the parakeet alias to its HF id, and reads the transcript from the output file. 4 new unit tests + full suite (1771) pass; live E2E confirmed memos transcribe/summarize/capture work (summarize/capture's AI step needs a working LLM provider — gemma4 is separately broken). Legacy pre-0.4.2 path preserved.

title: notes list pagination silently loses everything past 500 notes
description: NotesCommand.List.runPaginated (pippin/Commands/NotesCommand.swift:71-82) over-fetches offset+pageSize+1 notes and slices in-memory, but NotesBridge.listNotes (NotesBridge.swift) hard-clamps the fetch to min(limit, 500). Once offset+pageSize+1 > 500 on a vault with >500 notes, the bridge returns at most 500, Pagination.paginate slices past the end → empty page with no next_cursor. Notes 501+ are permanently unreachable AND pagination falsely reports 'done'. The proper fix is native offset support in the JXA list script (the bridge has no offset, hence over-fetch+slice). Interim low-risk mitigation: when the requested window exceeds the 500 ceiling AND the fetch came back full (>=500), throw a clear error ('narrow with --folder') instead of silently returning empty.
close_reason: Notes paginated path now detects when the requested window (offset+pageSize+1) exceeds NotesBridge.maxListLimit AND the fetch came back full — throws a clear ValidationError ('narrow with --folder') instead of silently slicing past a truncated 500-note fetch and falsely reporting done. Added named maxListLimit constant shared by the clamp and the guard. Proper native-JXA-offset pagination tracked as follow-up (pippin-pag).

title: calendar show/edit/delete by ID prefix fails for recurring events
description: CalendarBridge.findEventByPrefix (pippin/CalendarBridge/CalendarBridge.swift:235-249): when the exact-identifier lookup misses, it filters store.events(matching:) by calendarItemIdentifier.hasPrefix(id) and returns a match only if matches.count == 1. Every occurrence of a recurring event is a separate EKEvent sharing the SAME calendarItemIdentifier, so the count is >1 and the function returns nil. Result: 'pippin calendar show/edit/delete <prefix>' fails with eventNotFound for ANY repeating event — yet the 8-char prefix it rejects is exactly what 'calendar events' prints. Fix: dedupe matches by calendarItemIdentifier; accept when exactly one DISTINCT identifier matches (return matches.first), treat >1 distinct ids as genuinely ambiguous (nil).
close_reason: findEventByPrefix now dedupes matches by distinct calendarItemIdentifier (extracted to testable isUnambiguousPrefixMatch): recurring events (N occurrences, 1 identifier) resolve to their first occurrence; >1 distinct identifiers stay ambiguous. 4 unit tests added. Fixes calendar show/edit/delete <prefix> for repeating events.

title: mail list/list-paginated: MailBridge.listMessages runs on cooperative pool (missing detachBlocking)
description: MailList.run (pippin/Commands/MailCommand.swift:329) and runPaginated (:410) call MailBridge.listMessages — a blocking osascript subprocess, up to 60s under cross-account scaling — directly on the cooperative thread before the AI triage hop at :345. The subprocess wait is NOT wrapped in detachBlocking, so under mcp-server fanout 'mail list' parks a cooperative thread for the full list duration. Same class as pippin-56k/7h4 and HIGHER impact: mail list is the most-invoked tool (morning briefing calls it per-account). Fix: hop the listMessages call (and the subsequent processing that depends on it) through detachBlocking, matching MailList's own AI hop and the MailSearch pattern at :156.
close_reason: Wrapped listMessages + readMessage scan loops in detachBlocking; build + 221 Mail tests green

title: BatchCommand.dispatch blocks cooperative pool — runChild waitUntilExit() not hopped via detachBlocking
description: BatchCommand.dispatch (pippin/Commands/BatchCommand.swift:94-122) schedules runOne() inside withTaskGroup with N concurrency lanes. runOne (line 124) calls MCPServerRuntime.runChild which blocks on process.waitUntilExit() (MCPServerRuntime.swift). No detachBlocking hop. With --concurrency N, N cooperative threads park on subprocess waits simultaneously, starving the cooperative pool — exactly the anti-pattern DetachBlocking.swift exists to prevent. Fix: wrap runOne body in 'await detachBlocking { ... }' inside addTask.
close_reason: Wrapped runChild and mailai scan/triage/extract blocking bodies in detachBlocking; build + tests green

title: mailai scan/triage/extract block cooperative pool — missing detachBlocking hop
description: MailScan.run (pippin/Commands/MailAICommand.swift:157-184), MailTriage.run (~229-273), and the extract command (~304-333) call blocking sync work directly on the cooperative thread: MailBridge.readMessage/readMessages (osascript subprocess waitUntilExit) and PromptInjectionScanner.scanWithAI / TriageEngine / ActionExtractor (AI provider -> sendSynchronousRequest DispatchSemaphore.wait). Sibling commands (MailCommand, SummarizeCommand, CalendarCommand, RemindersCommand) all hop via detachBlocking; these three are the odd ones out. Wrap blocking bodies in 'try await detachBlocking { ... }'.
close_reason: Wrapped runChild and mailai scan/triage/extract blocking bodies in detachBlocking; build + tests green

title: contacts_search / contacts_list can hang MCP clients on large stores

Default softTimeoutMs=22000, clamp [1000, 300000]. Emit warnings via OutputOptions.emit(...,timedOut:,timedOutHint:). Ships in v0.20.2.

title: mail_activity times out in MCP clients — 120s script timeout exceeds 60s MCP cap
description:
mcp__pippin__mail_activity hangs past the 60s MCP runChild hard cap on every call, reported 3 consecutive evenings (2026-04-21/22/23) from the wfm-evening-priority-brief scheduled task. Reproduced with `{since: 2026-04-22, limit: 30, preview: 300}` and inside batch.

Root cause (verified in pippin/MailBridge/MailBridge.swift:36 and MailBridgeScripts.swift:303):
1. listActivity uses ScriptRunner timeoutSeconds=120 when preview>0 — **exceeds** the 60s MCP runChild hard cap in pippin/MCP/MCPServerRuntime.swift. The child process is reliably SIGTERM-killed before the JXA script returns, so MCP callers always get the childTimedOut error after exactly 60s.
2. buildActivityScript has no internal time check. It walks every target mailbox (default [INBOX, Sent]) per account, scans up to 500 msgs each, dedups, sorts, then for the preview pass calls `results[p].__msg.content()` per survivor — another N IMAP body fetches on iCloud. No early-break.
3. No ActivityOutcome / timedOut surfacing path.

Fix (apply the pippin-v1e pattern):
- buildActivityScript: accept softTimeoutMs (default ~45000 since activity does 2+ mailboxes; clamp 1s–5min). Guard the outer accounts × targetNames × messages loop AND the post-slice preview loop. Emit `{results, meta: {…, timedOut}}` instead of bare array.
- MailBridge.listActivity: return ActivityOutcome { messages, timedOut }. Lower ScriptRunner timeout to 55s (below the MCP 60s cap, above the 45s soft cap).
- MailActivityCommand: reuse/mirror MailCommand.Search.emitResult helpers — stderr warning, text trailer, envelope warnings.
- Tests: ActivityMetaTests, backward-compat decode, script-builder snippet assertions.

Secondary: even without the soft-timeout, lowering the ScriptRunner timeout below 60s is a standalone bug fix — right now every preview-on activity call is guaranteed to be killed by MCP runChild.
acceptance_criteria:
- mcp__pippin__mail_activity returns within ~50s on a 2-mailbox iCloud account instead of hitting the 60s MCP cap.
- When soft cap fires, envelope includes `warnings: [...]` and partial results under `.data`.
- ScriptRunner timeout strictly less than MCP runChild hard cap (60s).
- Full test suite green.
notes: Reference implementation: pippin-v1e (7bb7c54) + refactor 6bb02ba. MCP tool registered at pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift:194. MailActivityCommand at pippin/Commands/MailActivityCommand.swift.
close_reason: Fixed by commit dafe08e 'fix(mail): bound mail_list and mail_activity for MCP clients (#13)'. Both now apply the pippin-v1e soft-timeout pattern: JXA internal time check (_listStart/_activityStart) emitting meta.timedOut, ScanOutcome{messages,timedOut}, mcpHardTimeout clamping ScriptRunner caps to 55s under MCP (below the 60s runChild cap), and command-level timedOutHint warnings (MailCommand.List + MailActivityCommand). Verified: 41 tests green across MailBridgeListMetaTests/MailBridgeActivityMetaTests/MailBridgeHardTimeoutTests/MailBridgeSearchMetaTests/MailActivityTests. Issues were stale-open — fix landed but never closed.

title: mail_list times out in MCP clients — apply pippin-v1e soft-timeout pattern
description:
mcp__pippin__mail_list hangs past the 60s MCP runChild hard cap on every call, reported 3 consecutive evenings (2026-04-21/22/23) from the wfm-evening-priority-brief scheduled task. Reproduced with `{limit: 20, preview: 150}` and with no args. mail_search (fixed via pippin-v1e) returns fine; mail_list and mail_activity (this bug + pippin-NEXT) still hang.

Root cause (verified in pippin/MailBridge/MailBridge.swift:21 and MailBridgeScripts.swift:6):
1. buildListScript has no internal time check — it loops every account/mailbox/message with msg.content() when preview>0 (forces per-message IMAP body fetch).
2. listMessages uses ScriptRunner timeoutSeconds=60 when preview>0 (10s otherwise). The 60s matches the MCP runChild hard cap (pippin/MCP/MCPServerRuntime.swift), so any slow mailbox hits the cap and returns zero data.
3. No partial-results SearchOutcome analogue — no way to return what was collected + a timedOut warning.

Fix (apply the exact pippin-v1e pattern):
- buildListScript: accept softTimeoutMs (default ~18000, clamped 1s–5min). Wrap the outer accounts/messages loop with an early-break on `Date.now() - _listStart > softTimeoutMs`, set `_meta.timedOut = true`, emit `{results, meta}` instead of a bare array.
- MailBridge.listMessages: return a ListOutcome { messages, timedOut } (or reuse SearchOutcome shape). Lower ScriptRunner timeout to 30s so soft cap fires first.
- MailCommand.List: surface timedOut via the same emitResult/emitMessages/emitPage helpers introduced in 6bb02ba — stderr warning, text trailer, envelope-level `warnings: [...]` in agent mode.
- Tests: mirror MailBridgeSearchMetaTests + JXAScriptBuilderTests — backward-compat decode for old bare-array responses, script-builder snippet assertions.
acceptance_criteria:
- mcp__pippin__mail_list returns within ~25s on large iCloud inboxes instead of hitting the 60s MCP cap.
- When soft cap fires, agent envelope includes `warnings: ["mail list timed out; results may be incomplete"]` and partial results under `.data`.
- text mode: stderr warning + trailer when --verbose.
- Existing non-preview callers unaffected (10s path unchanged).
- Full test suite green.
notes: Reference implementation: commit 7bb7c54 (pippin-v1e) + 6bb02ba refactor. Reporter: automated scheduled task wfm-evening-priority-brief. See bug report in session history 2026-04-23. MCP tool registered at pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift:172.
close_reason: Fixed by commit dafe08e 'fix(mail): bound mail_list and mail_activity for MCP clients (#13)'. Both now apply the pippin-v1e soft-timeout pattern: JXA internal time check (_listStart/_activityStart) emitting meta.timedOut, ScanOutcome{messages,timedOut}, mcpHardTimeout clamping ScriptRunner caps to 55s under MCP (below the 60s runChild cap), and command-level timedOutHint warnings (MailCommand.List + MailActivityCommand). Verified: 41 tests green across MailBridgeListMetaTests/MailBridgeActivityMetaTests/MailBridgeHardTimeoutTests/MailBridgeSearchMetaTests/MailActivityTests. Issues were stale-open — fix landed but never closed.

title: mail attachments: -10000 / -1728 errors — wrong mailbox + unsafe save target
description:
pippin mail attachments <compound-id> fails on Gmail accounts in Mail.app for two distinct reasons:

1) **Wrong mailbox lookup (-1728).** The compound id format 'account||mailbox||num' embeds the Gmail *label* (e.g. 'Important', 'Starred'). pippin's AppleScript does 'mailbox "Important" of account <acc>' which errors -1728 because Gmail labels aren't addressable as mailboxes in Mail.app — the actual on-disk mailbox is INBOX. Fix: if the initial mailbox lookup returns -1728, fall back to scanning 'every message of mailbox "INBOX" of account <acc>' and filter by id/message-id/sender.

2) **Save target not pre-created (-10000).** Even once the message is found, 'save <mail attachment> in POSIX file "/path/to/file"' errors with 'Can't get POSIX file …' unless the target file already exists. Fix: before the save call, 'do shell script "touch" & quoted form of targetPath' (or the Swift equivalent). This is what finally unblocked a real extraction run on 2026-04-19.

**Evidence.** Reproduced against 'matthewwagner038@gmail.com||Important||6442'. 'pippin mail show' on the same compound id works fine (body + headers returned), so the issue is specifically in the attachments sub-command's AppleScript construction. Workaround (direct osascript) successfully extracted 16 attachments from the Shternie Bulua thread on 2026-04-19. Full workaround and the working script shape is documented at ~/.openclaw/workspace/MAIL_ATTACHMENTS_WORKAROUND.md.

**Scope.**
- Ensure mailbox resolution degrades to INBOX (or iterates mailboxes) when the label-as-mailbox lookup fails.
- Pre-touch the save target file before the AppleScript 'save' call.
- Add a regression test against a Gmail account with a labeled message that has >=1 attachment.
close_reason: Fixed in fe45558: nested-mailbox fallback + pre-touch + JXA 'in:' key + mimeType() guard + source() for IMAP binaries. Verified against the reported repro (matthewwagner038@gmail.com||Important||6442 — 198k PDF saved). Regression tests added in JXAScriptBuilderTests.

title: Browser snapshot fails: page object undefined in persistent context
description:
## Bug

`pippin browser snapshot` crashes with:
```
Error: Browser script failed: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'snapshot')
```

## Root Cause

In `BrowserBridge.swift:buildSnapshotScript()`:

```javascript
const browser = await webkit.launchPersistentContext(session, { headless: true });
const page = browser.pages()[0] || await browser.newPage();
const snapshot = await page.accessibility.snapshot({ interestingOnly: false });
```

**Problem:** When snapshot is called on an existing session:
1. browser.pages()[0] may be undefined (no pages in context)
2. await browser.newPage() may not work correctly with persistent context
3. page remains undefined → crash on page.accessibility.snapshot()

## Reproduction

```bash
pippin browser open "https://example.com"
pippin browser snapshot  # Crashes
```

## Proposed Fix

Check if page exists and has valid URL before snapshotting. If no page exists, either:
- Return error 'No page loaded'
- Or open about:blank first before snapshotting
close_reason: Closed

title: messages show/search/list return no body text — attributedBody (typedstream) not decoded
description: REPORTED by Talia 2026-06-09 (~/Desktop/cowork/projects/Pippin/bugs/messages_show_missing_body.md). messages show returns metadata but no text. ROOT CAUSE confirmed: MessagesDatabase.swift queries only m.text (showConversation SQL line 249, searchMessages line 174, conversation last_text line 103), but on modern macOS the message.text column is NULL/empty and the body lives in message.attributedBody. EMPIRICAL (chat.db, last 200 msgs): 198 empty text, 200 have attributedBody, 198 empty-text-but-attributedBody-present. IMPORTANT CORRECTION to the report: the blob is a TYPEDSTREAM / NSArchiver archive (header 040b73747265616d = '..streamtyped', verified), NOT an NSKeyedArchiver bplist — so NSKeyedUnarchiver.unarchivedObject(ofClass:from:) will FAIL on it. Must decode with NSUnarchiver (deprecated but present; wrap in an ObjC @try/@catch shim for crash safety on malformed blobs) or a typedstream parser. FIX: add a decoder; for each message use COALESCE-style logic: prefer non-empty m.text, else decode attributedBody to plain string. Apply to showConversation (text per message + lastMessagePreview), searchMessages (also fix the WHERE m.text LIKE filter, which misses attributedBody-only messages), and conversation last_text preview. mail_show/reminders_list are unaffected (different storage). ACCEPTANCE: messages show returns text for recent messages; messages search matches bodies stored in attributedBody.
close_reason: Fixed in 2d3017d: decode message.attributedBody typedstream via NSUnarchiver (CTypedStreamDecode ObjC shim) when message.text is empty; applied to messages show, conversation preview, and search (two-query merge since SQLite can't LIKE into a BLOB). Verified on real chat.db (show 8/8 texts incl emoji/tapbacks; search finds attributedBody-only messages). 1854 tests green incl golden synthetic-typedstream decode. Follow-up pippin-wve (scan-cap visibility + Q2 early-exit).

title: Homebrew TCC grants are NOT persistent — Cellar path is versioned (corrects pippin-jt9 claim)
description: Discovered while fixing pippin-0vr (2026-06-09). TCC keys bare-CLI (non-.app) permission grants on the binary's resolved PATH + signature, not on the signing identity alone. /opt/homebrew/bin/pippin is a symlink into /opt/homebrew/Cellar/pippin/<VERSION>/bin/pippin (verified via lsof txt). Each brew upgrade changes the Cellar path, so the TCC grant is lost on every upgrade EVEN with stable Developer ID signing. This contradicts docs (docs/gotchas/permissions.md 'shared across both install paths' / release skill + pippin-jt9 'brew binaries get persistent grants'). What signing actually buys: cdhash-independent DR match at a STABLE path, so grants persist across rebuilds only when the path is fixed (e.g. ~/.local/bin/pippin from make install, a copied real file). DURABLE setup = stable path + signing (+ disclaim for cross-launcher, pippin-0vr). ACTIONS: (1) correct permissions.md + release skill to stop claiming brew persistence; document that ~/.local/bin (stable copied path) is the durable home and brew re-prompts on upgrade; (2) consider a brew postinstall that copies (not symlinks) to a stable path, or document the limitation; (3) Talia/agents should point at the stable path, not the versioned brew symlink.
notes: Doc corrections DONE 2026-06-09: pippin CLAUDE.md (CI section + Talia consumer), global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, docs/gotchas/permissions.md, docs/skills/release/SKILL.md (+ mw-plugins copy), mw-plugins pippin-cli skill permissions section, Homebrew formula comment all corrected to state brew TCC grants are NOT persistent (versioned Cellar path) and ~/.local/bin/pippin is the durable home. Remaining (optional): consider a brew postinstall that copies pippin to a stable path so brew users also get persistence.
close_reason: Won't-do the optional postinstall (2026-06-12). The substantive work — doc corrections stating brew TCC grants are NOT persistent (versioned Cellar path) and ~/.local/bin/pippin is the durable home — was completed 2026-06-09 across pippin CLAUDE.md, global CLAUDE.md, docs/gotchas/permissions.md, the release skill, the mw-plugins pippin-cli skill, and the Homebrew formula comment. The only remaining item was an OPTIONAL brew postinstall that copies pippin to a stable path. Declining it: (1) a postinstall cp into ~/.local/bin would SHADOW the brew binary on PATH (confusing) and clobber make-install'd copies; (2) it's moot for the primary setup (make install → ~/.local/bin; Talia points there); (3) Homebrew 6.0.1's tap-trust gate already makes brew installs fragile (build.rb fails even with trusted taps; needs HOMEBREW_NO_REQUIRE_TAP_TRUST=1) — an argument against more brew automation. The durable path + the docs telling users to use it are the real fix. Re-file if brew-only TCC persistence becomes a real user need.

title: Fast-fail Mail/Notes/Messages Automation when not authorized + non-interactive (post-disclaim)
description: After pippin disclaims TCC responsibility (pippin-0vr), pippin runs under its OWN identity for Apple Events (Automation) too. Until pippin's identity is granted Automation control of Mail/Notes/Messages, JXA calls BLOCK to the 22s soft-timeout instead of fast-failing (the Apple-Events analog of the EventKit hang fixed in pippin-0vr). Repro: CLIIntegrationTests jumped 5s->94s under disclaim purely from mail/notes automation timeouts. FIX: before running an osascript that controls a target app, check AEDeterminePermissionToAutomateTarget(askUserIfNeeded: PermissionPriming.canRequestAccess()) for that app's bundle id; if not authorized and we can't prompt, throw a fast access_denied with remediation instead of running the (blocking) script. Needs per-bridge target bundle ids threaded into ScriptRunner. Until then, the post-upgrade un-granted window is slow for mail/notes; mitigated in tests via PIPPIN_NO_DISCLAIM. Interactive Terminal use self-heals (the automation prompt appears on first call).
close_reason: Implemented AEDeterminePermissionToAutomateTarget pre-check in ScriptRunner; Mail/Notes/Messages fast-fail with access_denied + Automation remediation instead of 22s hang. Commit 1bba884.

title: EventKit (Reminders/Calendar/Contacts) consent tracks the launching process, not pippin's identity — cross-launcher grants don't transfer (Talia/Hermes)
description: REPRO (2026-06-08): Talia (Hermes gateway, a python LaunchAgent) runs '/opt/homebrew/bin/pippin reminders create ...' and gets access_denied, even though the same binary works from Matt's interactive Terminal. EVIDENCE: 'pippin permissions --status' for the Developer-ID-signed brew binary (com.mattwag05.pippin) reports Reminders/Calendar/Contacts = not_determined when run from a non-Terminal launcher (Claude Code Bash), while Terminal has a working grant. CONCLUSION: macOS TCC associates EventKit/Contacts consent with the RESPONSIBLE (launching) process, so a grant approved under Terminal does not transfer to pippin spawned under the Hermes gateway. This CONTRADICTS docs/gotchas/permissions.md which claims 'EventKit/Contacts key on pippin's own identity, so signing fixes their persistence' — signing gives a stable identity but does NOT make a Terminal grant apply to an agent-launched pippin. pippin's user-facing remediation is already correct ('attaches to the launching app'). FIXES TO CONSIDER: (a) correct permissions.md (done as a doc note); (b) durable infra fix — run the Hermes gateway as the signed Hermes.app (stable Developer ID launcher identity) and grant Hermes.app the EventKit perms once; (c) general pippin-side fix — re-exec self with responsibility_spawnattrs_setdisclaim so pippin becomes its own responsible process and its signed identity holds the grant regardless of launcher (risky: MCP stdio + private API + startup latency; needs design). Verify the model empirically (grant under Terminal, then test via a Hermes dispatch).
close_reason: Shipped v0.31.0 + verified end-to-end. pippin re-execs disclaimed (CDisclaimSpawn) so TCC keys on its own identity regardless of launcher; EventKit ensureAccess fast-fails when it can't prompt. Proven: ~/.local/bin/pippin (granted, stable path) creates reminders from a non-Terminal launcher (created the Nissan reminder). Talia repointed from versioned brew path to stable ~/.local/bin/pippin (durable). Follow-ups: pippin-qjf (automation fast-fail), pippin-6sf (brew versioned-path persistence docs).

title: doctor: add Messages (FDA) check + point not-determined to pippin permissions

title: agentErrorCode produces junk codes for foreign errors (NSError/URLError/DecodingError)
description: agentErrorCode(for:) camelToSnakeCases String(describing: error). For NSError/URLError/CocoaError this yields garbage like 'error _domain=_n_s_u_r_l_error_domain _code=-1001 "' in the agent envelope error.code contract field. Harden to emit a clean stable code (unknown_error) for non-enum-case descriptions; typed PippinLib codes unchanged.

title: release.yml consistently cancelled on GitHub macos-15 runners — releases must be published locally
description: WHY: The v0.28.0 release.yml run (and the 3 prior runs) all ended in conclusion=cancelled, not success. The 'Run tests' step (swift test on macos-15) is cancelled at the platform level (no timeout-minutes/concurrency configured in the workflow, so it's runner-provisioning/billing/queue, not our code — make ci passes locally with 0 failures). Because tests never finish, 'Build release tarball' + 'Create GitHub release' are skipped, so NO GitHub Release/tarball is published. This is the same slow-GitHub-hosted-macos-runner problem that already drove ci.yml to be disabled (see CLAUDE.md 'CI' section). WHAT: for v0.28.0 the GitHub release was published manually (make tarball + awk changelog extract + gh release create v0.28.0 --title 'v0.28.0 — pippin' --notes-file ... <tarball>) — see the updated release SKILL.md failure-recovery. Brew/Talia are unaffected because the Homebrew formula builds from source (tag+revision), not from the release tarball. FIX OPTIONS (pick one): (a) drop the 'swift test' step from release.yml so the runner only builds the tarball + creates the release fast (local 'make ci' before tag already gates correctness per the skill); (b) disable release.yml entirely and make 'publish release locally' the documented path (consistent with ci.yml); (c) move release.yml to the self-hosted forgejo 'macos' runner that already mirrors CI. Needs a decision — surface to Matt before changing CI infra.
close_reason: Disabled GitHub release.yml (gh workflow disable); GitHub releases now published locally per release skill step 6; forgejo release.yaml handles tailnet. Documented in CLAUDE.md CI section + skill.

Config via ai.openai.{baseURL,model,apiKey} or --provider/--model/--api-key. Optional key for local endpoints. Decouples summarize/capture/smart-create/actions/do/mail-ai-assisted from local Ollama. Motivated by gemma4:latest being non-functional in the local Ollama.

title: Notes scan soft-times-out to EMPTY: default 'notes list'/'notes search' return zero results without --folder on large libraries
description: WHY: On a large Notes library, the JXA enumeration can't return even a few notes within the 22s soft-timeout (SoftTimeout.defaultMs), so 'pippin notes list --limit 3' returns data:[] with warning 'Notes scan exceeded soft timeout, returning partial results — narrow with --folder or --limit'. 'partial' is empty, so the default listing is unusable and notes_show can't be chained from notes_list. Narrowing with --folder <name> returns results instantly and notes_show then works perfectly. WHAT: reproduced repeatedly — notes_list (no folder) -> 0 items; notes list --folder 'Admissions Guides' -> immediate result. AREA: NotesBridge JXA scan performance / ordering. IMPROVEMENT options: (a) stream/yield notes as found so the soft-timeout returns a non-empty prefix instead of nothing; (b) enumerate most-recently-modified first so a useful page returns before the cap; (c) raise/scale the soft timeout for Notes specifically; (d) at minimum, when partial==empty, make the warning explicit that ZERO notes were returned. Found during 44-tool MCP E2E (notes_show passes when given a valid id).
acceptance_criteria: A default 'pippin notes list --limit N' on a large library returns a non-empty prefix (or the warning explicitly states zero were enumerated), so an agent can chain notes_show without needing --folder.
close_reason: Closed

title: doctor's mlx-audio / AI-dependency checks are shallow — report 'available' while the actual invocation is broken
description: WHY: doctor reported 'mlx-audio: available, version 0.4.2' and exited all-green, yet memos transcribe/summarize/capture_to_reminders all fail because AudioBridge passes a CLI arg shape incompatible with mlx-audio 0.4.2 (see pippin-8ik). doctor only probes importability + version, never the call signature, so version skew that breaks the real invocation stays invisible. WHAT: a health check that validates the wrong layer gives false confidence. IMPROVEMENT: deepen the mlx-audio probe to validate the actual invocation contract — e.g. a fast 'arg-shape dry run' (invoke stt.generate --help and assert the flags pippin will pass exist), or a tiny canned-audio smoke transcription behind a --deep flag. Same principle applies to other AI deps (ollama model presence is checked, but not that summarize's prompt path works). Relates to pippin-8ik.
acceptance_criteria: doctor fails (or warns) when the installed mlx-audio CLI does not accept the flags AudioBridge will pass, so the transcription break in pippin-8ik would have been caught by doctor.
close_reason: Closed

title: agent/MCP --format swallows ArgumentParser ValidationError messages (generic 'CommandError error 1')
description: WHY: Pippin's MCP contract shells out to 'pippin <cmd> --format agent' for every tool call, so MCP/agent clients (the primary consumers) receive whatever agent-mode emits on error. When a command throws an ArgumentParser ValidationError, the human (default format) gets the actionable text but the agent format gets a useless generic envelope. WHAT: 'pippin calendar create --title X --start "2026-06-04 12:00"' prints 'Error: --start must be in YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601 format.' in default format, but '--format agent' returns {error:{code:command_error, message:'The operation couldn-t be completed. (ArgumentParser.CommandError error 1.)'}}. The remediation message is lost. Likely systemic to all ValidationErrors in agent mode (the catch site renders ArgumentParser errors generically instead of forwarding error.message / building a remediation block). FIX: in the agent-mode error path, surface the ArgumentParser ValidationError's message (and ideally a remediation hint) into the envelope's error.message/remediation rather than the opaque 'CommandError error N'. Found during 44-tool MCP E2E.
acceptance_criteria: calendar_create with a malformed --start in --format agent returns error.message containing the YYYY-MM-DD/ISO-8601 guidance; a regression test asserts a ValidationError's text survives into the agent envelope.
close_reason: Closed

title: Mail body cache at readMessage seam (mitigate repeat-fetch cost)
description: CONFIRMED root cause of Mail timeouts: msg.content() per-message IMAP body download via JXA. readMessage is the single Swift seam for full-body fetch and mail index calls it per-message (documented N+1). Add MailBodyCache (GRDB, ~/.config/pippin/mail-cache.db, compound_id PRIMARY KEY → cached MailMessage JSON + cached_at). readMessage gains read-through/write-through (compound-id is immutable per body, so safe). Wins: mail index re-runs skip re-fetch; repeated mail show is instant; mail cache warm pre-populates. mail list/search stay UNCACHED → read/unread metadata always live. New subcommands: mail cache stats|clear|warm; --no-cache escape on mail show. NOTE: the bulk list --preview / search --body timeout path fetches bodies INSIDE the JXA loop — caching that needs a fetch-metadata-then-cache-per-body restructure (slower cold, big change to load-bearing JXA) → deferred follow-up.
close_reason: Shipped: MailBodyCache at readMessage seam; cache hit ~75x faster (1.3s→17ms); mail cache stats/clear/warm + mail show --no-cache

title: Make --fields projection work in agent mode + consolidate + add to mail list

This phase: (1) shared FieldProjection helper (parse + project array/single/paginated-items shapes); (2) agent-mode envelope honors --fields via printAgentProjectedJSON + OutputOptions.printAgent/emit fields param (resolves the documented emit-drops-fields gotcha for agent mode); (3) dedup ReminderModels/CalendarModels jsonData + NotesCommand filteredNoteDicts onto the shared helper (json output byte-identical); (4) wire agent branches in Notes/Calendar/Reminders list/search; (5) add --fields to mail list (json+agent) + mail_list MCP tool. Deferred follow-up: lift --fields into OutputOptions universally (higher json-output-churn risk); add to mail search/activity.

New pure helper PippinExitCode.swift. Lets calling shells branch on failure class without parsing JSON. Envelope JSON unchanged. title: Flaky: JobStoreTests.testJobIdGeneratesUniqueValues (~0.5% birthday collision)
description: testJobIdGeneratesUniqueValues generates 100 JobId.generate() values in a single millisecond and asserts all 100 are unique. JobId is an 11-hex ms-timestamp prefix + 5-hex (20-bit) random suffix (Job.swift:100-104), so 100 same-ms IDs collide with birthday probability ~100^2/(2*2^20) ~= 0.5% per run. Observed failing once during a full make ci run ('got 99 unique'), green on 3/3 isolated retries. The ID generator is fine (documented ~1-in-1M for same-ms spawns; real spawns are seconds apart) — the TEST over-asserts. Fix: make the test match the documented guarantee (e.g. mock/space timestamps, or reduce the same-ms sample, or assert suffix entropy) instead of requiring 100 same-ms IDs to be collision-free.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.24.2: rewrote testJobIdGeneratesUniqueValues to space generations past the 1ms boundary + constant-suffix guard; verified 0 failures across 100 runs.

title: reminders --due-before/--due-after include undated reminders
description: RemindersBridge.listReminders dueBefore/dueAfter filters return true (include) for a reminder with no dueDateComponents, so undated reminders pollute every date-filtered query and appear in BOTH due-before X and due-after X. Inconsistent with createdAfter/modifiedAfter which return false (exclude). Fix: return false for missing due date.
close_reason: reminders due-before/after now exclude undated reminders (passesDueFilters); digest upcoming window anchored to endOfDay (full N days) + calendar-days bounded 1-366. 5 tests. make ci green (1717 tests). Pushed 0c87069.

title: JSONValue.intValue traps on out-of-Int64-range double (MCP arg crash)
description: JSONValue decodes a JSON number exceeding Int64 as .double; intValue then does Int64(value) which TRAPS (crash) for out-of-range/non-finite doubles. Any int-typed MCP tool arg (limit/page/sinceHours/timeout/mailLimit) with a huge value like 1e19 or 99999999999999999999 crashes the MCP child process. Same Int64(Double) trap class as appleNanos. Fix: guard isFinite + Int64 range, return nil (treat as absent) instead of trapping.
close_reason: intValue now guards isFinite + Int64 range before Int64(Double); out-of-range MCP int args (1e19, huge literals) return nil (absent→default) instead of trapping the child. 8 unit tests incl. end-to-end decode→intValue. make ci green (1712 tests). Pushed 4a00c4d.

title: MCP tool argv: dash-prefixed option values misparsed as flags
description: ToolRegistry emitted option values as two argv tokens [--flag, value]. An MCP-client-supplied value starting with '-' (search body '-19%', markdown-bullet note/reminder title '- item') was misparsed by ArgumentParser as a stray flag, failing the whole tool call (CommandError). Confirmed: 'pippin calendar events --range -x' fails, '--range=-x' works. Fixed: ArgHelpers.option() binds --flag=value; optionIfString/optionIfInt + 8 inline option sites converted.
close_reason: ArgHelpers.option() binds --flag=value; optionIfString/optionIfInt + 8 inline option sites converted. Dash-prefixed option values (search bodies, bullet titles) no longer misparsed as flags. 3 new tests + updated argv-shape assertions. Positional case → pippin-bhn. make ci green (1702 tests). Pushed d78d34d.

title: Pagination overflow trap: offset + pageSize crashes on huge --page-size/cursor
description: Cursor.resolve guards pageSize>0 but not an upper bound. paginate() does min(all.count, safeOffset+pageSize) and pageFromPushdown() does offset+pageSize — both overflow-trap (crash) when --page-size is near Int.max (with a cursor offset>=1) or a crafted cursor carries offset near Int.max. pageSize+1 fetch in pushdown bridges traps too. Same overflow class as parseRange/appleNanos/Messages-limit. Fix: cap pageSize in resolve + overflow-safe offset arithmetic.
close_reason: resolve() caps pageSize at 100000; paginate() uses remaining-bounded take (no offset+pageSize overflow); pageFromPushdown() uses addingReportingOverflow → drops next cursor on overflow. 4 regression tests. make ci green (1692 tests). Pushed 3fca206.

title: MessagesDatabase --limit unbounded: overflow trap + unbounded SQLite fetch
description: limit:Int=50 @Option unbounded. limit=Int.max → limit+1 (showConversation) and limit+excluded.count (list/search) overflow-trap crash. limit=-1 → SQLite LIMIT -1 unbounded fetch (OOM on large Messages DB). Fixed: MessagesDatabase.clampLimit normalizes to [0,100000] at all three query method entry points.
close_reason: clampLimit() normalizes to [0,100000] at all three query method entry points; negative→0 (no unbounded fetch), Int.max→100000 (no overflow trap). 5 regression tests. make ci green (1686 tests). Pushed b359ba3.

title: EmbeddingProvider ignores MCP timeout budget → SIGKILL past 60s child cap
description: OllamaEmbeddingProvider.embed/embedBatch hardcoded 120s/300s timeouts and ignored isMCPContext(), unlike Ollama/Claude chat providers which use aiRequestTimeoutSeconds(). Under PIPPIN_MCP=1 a slow embedding server blows past MCPServerRuntime's 60s child cap → SIGKILL misreported as protocol error. Fixed via single-source requestTimeout(batch:) helper: MCP→50s, CLI→120s single/300s batch. waitTimeoutSeconds now timeout+5.
close_reason: requestTimeout(batch:) single-source helper: MCP→50s (under 60s child cap), CLI→120s/300s. Both call sites use waitTimeoutSeconds=timeout+5. 2 regression tests. make ci green (1681 tests). Pushed b2f2783.

title: VoiceMemo.init(row:) + deleteMemo crash on NULL Core Data columns
description: GRDB traps when row[col] non-optional decode hits NULL. Apple Voice Memos DB stores NULL ZPATH/ZDURATION for not-yet-downloaded iCloud recordings — one such row crashed entire memos list. deleteMemo also force-decoded ZPATH and an empty path would make appendingPathComponent return dir itself, deleting the whole recordings folder. Fixed: optional decode + fallbacks in VoiceMemo.init; optional ZPATH + empty-path guard in deleteMemo.
close_reason: VoiceMemo.init(row:) and deleteMemo now decode NULL Core Data columns optionally with fallbacks; deleteMemo guards empty-path to avoid deleting recordings dir. 3 regression tests, make ci green (1679 tests), pushed 0b7ed13.

title: Harden parseRange + appleNanos against overflow crashes on user input
description: parseRange('today+<huge N>') trapped on n+1 (Int.max) overflow and force-unwrapped Calendar.date(byAdding:) returning nil — reachable from --range flag. appleNanos(from:) trapped on Int64(Double) for far-future dates reachable from message --since. Both hardened: parseRange caps N at 3660 days + guard-lets; appleNanos clamps to Int64 bounds.
close_reason: Hardened parseRange (cap N at 3660 days + guard-let, fixes today+Int.max overflow trap) and appleNanos (clamp to Int64 bounds). 5 regression tests added, make ci green, pushed 32a6725.

title: contacts search --email hides matched address when --fields omits emails
description: ContactsBridge.searchByEmail (pippin/ContactsBridge/ContactsBridge.swift) FETCHES emails via keysForFields(forceEmails:true) to match, but calls convert(contact, fields:) WITHOUT forcing emails into the output. With e.g. --fields id,fullName the matched email is omitted, so the user sees matches with no indication of which address matched. Fix: add forceEmails param to convert (mirroring keysForFields) and pass forceEmails:true on the email-search path so the matched address is always emitted.
close_reason: Added forceEmails param to ContactsBridge.convert; searchByEmail passes forceEmails:true so the matched address is always emitted regardless of --fields. build + 1667 tests green.

title: contacts search: --limit ignored in non-paginated path
description: SearchContacts.run (pippin/Commands/ContactsCommand.swift:139) emitted the full result set with no prefix(limit) when pagination is inactive; --limit only took effect as defaultPageSize when --page-size/--cursor was supplied. Inconsistent with calendar events/search and notes search, whose --limit caps results unconditionally. Fix: apply Array(contacts.prefix(limit)) in the non-paginated branch and align help text to 'Maximum results to return'.
close_reason: Non-paginated SearchContacts.run now applies Array(contacts.prefix(limit)); help text aligned to 'Maximum results to return'. Consistent with calendar/notes search.

title: Drift: canonical Swift/Xcode toolchain floor inconsistent across repo sources
description:
The minimum toolchain version is declared inconsistently:
- Package.swift:1 — swift-tools-version: 6.0 + Swift 6 language mode
- README.md:55 — source builds require Xcode 16+ / Swift 6.2+
- .github/copilot-instructions.md:7 — still says Swift 6.0

The repo needs ONE canonical minimum. Two repo-backed options:
(a) Keep Swift 6.0 as the floor and downgrade the README claim to match.
(b) Raise the package/tooling baseline to Swift 6.2+ / Xcode 16+ to match README.

Once chosen, align all declarations: Package.swift:1, README.md:55, .github/copilot-instructions.md:7, and any agent docs (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md) that mention a Swift/Xcode version. This is doc/config alignment, not a code change.
close_reason: Aligned all toolchain-floor declarations to Swift 6.0 — the machine-enforced floor in Package.swift (swift-tools-version: 6.0 + .swiftLanguageMode(.v6)). Chose option (a): README.md:55 and :384 changed from 'Swift 6.2+' to 'Swift 6.0+' (README's prior 'Xcode 16+ / Swift 6.2+' was internally inconsistent — Xcode 16.0 ships Swift 6.0). copilot-instructions.md already said 6.0 (now correct, not wrong). Package.swift unchanged (canonical). Pure doc alignment, no code change. CI uses latest-stable Xcode so no minimum enforced there.

title: Drift: CI Xcode setup duplicated and not aligned across workflows
description:
setup-xcode usage is inconsistent across CI definitions:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml:19, codeql.yml:30, release.yml:15 — all use setup-xcode with xcode-version: latest-stable
- .github/copilot-setup-steps.yml:6 — pins a DIFFERENT setup-xcode SHA
- .forgejo/workflows/ci.yaml:17 — does NOT set up Xcode at all

Normalize: use one setup-xcode action pin (full commit SHA per the repo's action-pinning policy) everywhere, and decide whether the Forgejo workflow should also explicitly install/select Xcode to match GitHub Actions behavior (or document why it intentionally differs). Depends on the toolchain-floor decision (which Xcode version to pin to).
close_reason: Normalized setup-xcode pins: .github/copilot-setup-steps.yml was pinning an older SHA (60606e2 # v1) vs the three main workflows (ed7a3b1 # v1.7.0); aligned it to ed7a3b1 so all four GitHub usages share one pin (satisfies action-pinning policy). Forgejo (.forgejo/workflows/ci.yaml) intentionally omits setup-xcode — it runs on a self-hosted macos runner with system-selected Xcode and the action is unavailable in Forgejo's registry; documented this with an inline NOTE rather than adding a no-op step. YAML validated.

title: Add CI grep-lint: flag MailBridge.(read|list|search) / runChild not lexically inside detachBlocking
description: Caller-side detachBlocking is the established (and structurally forced) convention, but it is forget-prone — pippin-56k/7h4 plus the newly-found MailCommand/MailIndex/DoCommand gaps prove coverage is incomplete. A structural chokepoint isn't available (runChild is sync, called from McpServerCommand's sync readLine loop; ArgumentParser owns command dispatch). The realistic durable guard is a grep-based CI lint that flags blocking bridge calls (MailBridge.readMessage/listMessages/searchMessages, MCPServerRuntime.runChild) and DispatchSemaphore.wait/sendSynchronousRequest in async command files that are not lexically enclosed by a detachBlocking { } block. Wire into .github/workflows/ci.yml.
close_reason: Added scripts/lint-detach-blocking.py — an async-aware brace-stack analyzer that flags MailBridge.(read|list|search)* / MCPServerRuntime.runChild / sendSynchronousRequest / DispatchSemaphore calls inside async function bodies not lexically enclosed by detachBlocking { }. Strips comments/strings, handles same-line wraps, ignores sync helper funcs, supports // detach-lint:allow suppression, ships 8 self-test fixtures (--self-test). Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml. Running it surfaced 10 real cooperative-thread-blocking gaps (MailCommand accounts/mailboxes/search×2/show/attachments, MailActivityCommand, DigestCommand×2, MailWatchCommand×2) — all fixed by wrapping in detachBlocking per the established pattern. Build + 1669 tests green.

title: mailai index: listMessages + readMessage loop run on cooperative pool (missing detachBlocking)
description: MailIndex.run (pippin/Commands/MailAICommand.swift:45 listMessages, :77 per-message readMessage loop) runs blocking osascript subprocesses directly on the cooperative thread; only the later embed phase (~:92) hops correctly via continuation+DispatchQueue.global. Under mcp-server fanout the index read phase parks a cooperative thread. Fix: wrap the listMessages + readMessage read phase in detachBlocking. Also consider that the readMessage loop is sequential — could parallelize like BatchCommand if it becomes a hot path (lower priority).
close_reason: Wrapped listMessages + readMessage scan loops in detachBlocking; build + 221 Mail tests green

title: searchMessages timeout drifted from documented contract; single-account --body 75s exceeds 60s MCP runChild cap
description: MailBridge.searchMessages (pippin/MailBridge/MailBridge.swift:100-104) computes baseTimeout = crossAccount ? 50 : 30, then searchBody ? base+45 : base+15 — yielding single-account 45s (no body) and 75s (with body), not the 30/45 stated in the inline comment and CLAUDE.md. The 75s single-account --body cap EXCEEDS the 60s MCPServerRuntime.defaultChildTimeoutSeconds, so under MCP a slow single-account body search is SIGKILLed by the parent before the bridge can return partial results. Fix: reconcile arithmetic with the documented contract and ensure no path exceeds the 60s runChild cap under MCP.
close_reason: Added MailBridge.clampHardTimeout: under MCP, list/activity/search hard caps clamp to 55s (below 60s runChild cap) so wedged osascript self-reaps gracefully. CLI keeps full scaled values. 4 unit tests added.

title: AudioConverter.convertToWAV16kMono drops audio tail — converter never flushed after resample
description: AudioConverter.convertToWAV16kMono (pippin/MemosBridge/AudioConverter.swift:93-122) feeds one input buffer per converter.convert call then returns .noDataNow, but never flushes the converter after the input loop. AVAudioConverter buffers samples internally during sample-rate conversion (e.g. 44.1kHz->16kHz); without a final drain pass feeding .endOfStream until output empties, trailing buffered samples are discarded — non-native audio files lose the final fraction of a second before transcription. Native .m4a skips conversion so common case is unaffected. Fix: add a drain loop after the input loop feeding .endOfStream until outBuffer.frameLength==0.
close_reason: Investigated rigorously — NOT a real bug. With a properly-flushed input (44100 frames), the existing converter produces 15994 output frames at 16kHz: a 6-frame (0.375ms) tail, within resampler rounding. The reported '~72ms tail loss' was a measurement artifact (the diagnostic read the input file before its AVAudioFile writer flushed its final ~3140-frame buffer). The canonical .endOfStream-drain rewrite was prototyped and measured: it changes output by ~6 frames (sub-ms) since AVAudioConverter priming is 0/0 for LPCM input regardless of source codec (AVAudioFile decodes compressed formats to LPCM before the converter sees them). Not worth rewriting delicate working AVFoundation code for a sub-ms gain. Added a duration-preservation regression test (testConvertPreservesDuration) to guard against any genuine future truncation.

title: RemindersBridge.fetchRemindersSync — unbounded semaphore.wait() on cooperative thread, no timeout
description: RemindersBridge.fetchRemindersSync (pippin/RemindersBridge/RemindersBridge.swift:310-319) does a bare semaphore.wait() (no timeout) on the cooperative thread; reached by listReminders/searchReminders/findReminderByPrefix, and RemindersCommand.run calls these without detachBlocking. Two issues: (a) cooperative-pool blocking contract violation; (b) no timeout — if EventKit fetchReminders callback never fires the call hangs forever. Contrast StatusCommand.gatherRemindersStatus which uses the same pattern with a 5s bounded wait inside detachBlocking. Fix: hop through detachBlocking and bound the wait.
close_reason: Bounded fetchRemindersSync wait at 15s (was unbounded -> infinite-hang risk); returns partial results + stderr warning on timeout. The cooperative-pool hop is the harder structural fix (EKReminder/EKEventStore are non-Sendable) and is tracked by pippin-91n (EventKit withTimeout wrapper).

title: Notes JXA: pre-loop fetch+sort can exceed ScriptRunner cap on large vaults
description: In NotesBridge.buildSearchScript and buildListScript, app.notes() (or folder.notes()) + .slice().sort() runs BEFORE the soft-timeout-protected loop. On vaults with hundreds of notes this fetch+sort alone can take 30s+, hitting the ScriptRunner 30s hard cap before the loop's soft cap can fire. Result: user gets typed NotesBridgeError.timeout instead of the friendlier 'partial results' warning, and zero data is returned. Fix sketch: hoist the time check in front of the sort, so if pre-loop work alone exceeds softTimeoutMs we set _meta.timedOut=true and return empty results with the warning. Reference: confirmed via manual repro on a 440-note vault — 'pippin notes search test --limit 3 --format agent' returns timeout error, not soft-timeout warning.
close_reason: Hardened NotesBridge buildListScript/buildSearchScript against the pre-existing sort cost. The earlier pre-sort time check (b53de65) only guarded a slow app.notes() FETCH; the dominant cost — a comparator firing an Apple Event per comparison (O(n log n) round-trips) — ran uninterruptibly and could blow the 35s ScriptRunner cap before any partial result returned. Fix: materialize each note's modificationDate exactly once into a time-checked JS array, then sort in pure JS (O(n) Apple Events, self-bounding via soft cap → partial results + timedOut=true). Extracted into shared jsSortNotesByModDate helper; results loop reuses cached ISO date. Tests: 4 regression assertions added; full suite 1669 green, lint clean.

title: Notes list: add JXA soft timeout (mail_search analogue)
description: NotesBridge.listNotes fetches all notes, sorts by modificationDate, then calls .body() + .plaintext() on each in the slice. No internal time check; large vaults can exceed MCP timeout. Add 22s soft timeout inside the .map over sliced notes. Wrap response in {results, meta:{timedOut}}.
close_reason: Shipped in dd7b487: NotesBridge searchNotes/listNotes/listFolders return Outcome<T> with 22s soft timeout in JXA loop; surfaced via shared OutputOptions.emit() helper to stderr/agent/text. Mail-search analogue applied across all three Notes JXA loops.

title: Notes search: add JXA soft timeout (mail_search analogue)
description: NotesBridge.searchNotes iterates every note in the folder/app, calls .plaintext() per note, and has no internal time check. Identical shape to the recently-fixed mail_search bug. Add 22s soft timeout inside the JXA loop, return Outcome { result, timedOut }, surface meta.timedOut in the agent envelope warnings.
close_reason: Shipped in dd7b487: NotesBridge searchNotes/listNotes/listFolders return Outcome<T> with 22s soft timeout in JXA loop; surfaced via shared OutputOptions.emit() helper to stderr/agent/text. Mail-search analogue applied across all three Notes JXA loops.

title: Fix process.waitUntilExit() blocking cooperative thread in DoctorCommand and AudioBridge
description:
DoctorCommand.run() and AudioCommand.run() are declared async throws. They call process.waitUntilExit() directly on the cooperative thread pool, blocking threads for seconds to minutes.

DoctorCommand: checkNotesAutomation() (~line 335), python3-check helper (~line 460)
AudioBridge: runProcess() (~line 407)

Fix: Wrap Process.waitUntilExit() in withCheckedThrowingContinuation + DispatchQueue.global().async, or mark helpers @concurrent (Swift 6.2).

Severity: High — blocks cooperative threads under any concurrent usage
close_reason: DoctorCommand.run already hops runAllChecks() via detachBlocking (line 46). The live cooperative-pool blocker was AudioCommand's guard AudioBridge.isAvailable() (4 sites), which probes via which + 'python3 -c import mlx_audio' subprocesses (~1-2s Python startup) BEFORE the detachBlocking — now hopped via 'await detachBlocking { AudioBridge.isAvailable() }'. Transcriber's isAvailable runs inside the already-hopped summarize/batch chains. Full suite 1662 tests green.

title: Audit OllamaEmbeddingProvider: embed/embedBatch block cooperative thread via DispatchSemaphore
description:
OllamaEmbeddingProvider.embed and embedBatch are synchronous functions that internally call sendSynchronousRequest() which uses DispatchSemaphore.wait() with a 125s timeout.

Currently only called correctly via withCheckedThrowingContinuation + DispatchQueue.global().async in MailIndex, but the type contract doesn't enforce this. Any future async call site that forgets the GCD hop will block a cooperative thread for up to 125s.

Fix: Make embed/embedBatch async throws using URLSession.data(for:), or mark them @concurrent (Swift 6.2) to make the executor contract explicit.

File: pippin/MailAIBridge/EmbeddingProvider.swift, AIProvider/AIProvider.swift
close_reason: Audit complete: both callers of OllamaEmbeddingProvider.embed/embedBatch already hop off-pool — SemanticSearch.search (embed) runs inside detachBlocking at MailCommand.swift:156/232, and embedBatch runs inside the continuation+DispatchQueue.global hop in MailIndex (MailAICommand.swift:103). No on-pool blocking remains.

title: reminders_list needs created-after / modified-after recency filter
description:
Cowork feedback 2026-04-20: 'Reminders list has no recency filter. The full list came back with items going back to 2024, all mixed together. A createdAfter or modifiedAfter filter would make it much easier to focus on what is actually new or recently changed during an update scan.'

Current reminders_list (pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift:366) supports: list, completed, dueBefore, dueAfter, priority, limit. No filter by creation or modification date.

EKReminder exposes .creationDate (already used for sort at pippin/RemindersBridge/RemindersBridge.swift:98) and .lastModifiedDate — both available for filtering.

Add CLI flags --created-after / --modified-after (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601) to 'pippin reminders list' and surface them in the reminders_list MCP tool schema. Accept a relative shortcut like '7d' if the date-parse helper already supports it.

Acceptance: 'pippin reminders list --modified-after 2026-04-13' returns only reminders touched in the last week.
close_reason: Added --created-after and --modified-after to reminders list (CLI + MCP tool) filtering on EKReminder.creationDate / .lastModifiedDate. Mirrored existing dueBefore/dueAfter validation. Committed in 04bcf2e on branch claude/exciting-goldwasser-87467d.

title: mail_list returns metadata only — add body preview or optional body inclusion
description:
Cowork feedback 2026-04-20: 'Mail returns metadata only, no body. I could see subjects and senders but couldn't read content without fetching each email individually. For a comprehensive scan trying to surface action items and commitments, that is a significant limitation — I would need a lot of extra tool calls to actually read anything.'

Current shape: mail_list (pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift:172) returns MailMessage metadata. To read bodies, the agent must call mail_show per message — expensive for scans.

Proposals (pick one):
1. Add --preview N flag to mail list that returns first N chars of plain text body per message.
2. Add --with-body flag that includes full body (slow; caller opt-in).
3. Add a new mail_scan tool that returns subject + sender + body preview in a single call, optimized for agent scan workflows.

Note: mail_search already has --body, but that is a search predicate, not a return-body flag.

Acceptance: an agent doing a 'scan my inbox for commitments' task can do it in one tool call per mailbox, not N+1.
close_reason: Added --preview N flag to mail list (CLI + mail_list MCP tool). bodyPreview is opt-in (script unchanged when flag absent); when set, msg.content() triggers IMAP fetch, truncated with ellipsis at min(N, 4000). Timeout bumps to 60s with preview on. MailMessage.bodyPreview added as encodeIfPresent (back-compatible). Committed 4bebb0b (squash-merged in 0d827e0).

title: Auto-launch fallback for Apple apps when bridge times out
description:
Cowork feedback 2026-04-20: notes_list failed with 'Notes automation timed out — ensure Notes.app is running.' Notes was a blind spot for a comprehensive scan. User requested this fallback apply to ALL Apple-app-backed services (Notes, Mail, Calendar, Reminders, Contacts), not just Notes — any bridge that depends on a host app being running should auto-launch it (via 'open -a <AppName>' or equivalent) and retry once before surfacing the timeout error.

Candidate implementation: shared helper that wraps JXA/subprocess bridges. On timeout or 'application isn't running' style errors, invoke 'open -gja <App>' (background, activate) then re-run the script once. If it still fails, return the current error. Apps to cover: Notes.app, Mail.app, Calendar.app, Reminders.app, Contacts.app. Music.app/Podcasts not currently bridged.

Ref: pippin/NotesBridge/NotesBridge.swift:295 runScript, pippin/Models/NoteModels.swift:13 timeout message.
close_reason: Extracted shared ScriptRunner from NotesBridge/MailBridge; adds open -gja retry once on timeout when appName is provided. Scope narrowed to Notes+Mail (Calendar/Reminders/Contacts use EventKit/CNContactStore, no host app dep). 5 ScriptRunner tests + existing 38 Notes/Mail tests pass. Committed 4609088 (squash-merged in 0d827e0).

title: Register memos tools in MCP ToolRegistry
description: pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift registers 26 tools but excludes all memos subcommands per docs/mcp-server.md's 'heavy AI subsystems out of scope' policy. That's the wrong call for agentic use — memos is precisely what Claude agents want to reach. Register 5 memos tools that shell out to the existing --format agent CLI paths.

Each tool's argv ends in --format agent (enforced by existing testAllArgvEndWithFormatAgent lint test). 3. docs/mcp-server.md drops the blanket memos exclusion and documents the 5 new tools. 4. tools/list response over stdio includes the new tools.

title: Fix mlx-audio invocation + pin version
description: pippin invokes mlx-audio via 'python3 -m mlx_audio.stt' in AudioBridge.swift:64. mlx-audio 0.4.2 broke this by restructuring the module, producing 'No module named mlx_audio.stt.__main__'. Pippin has no pinned version anywhere. Switch to the entry-point binary with version-aware fallback, pin a known-good version, and have DoctorCommand report version mismatch cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. AudioBridge.swift resolves and invokes mlx_audio.stt.generate entry point (not -m module path) when available; falls back to -m only for older versions. 2. MLXAudioTranscriber.pinnedVersion constant documents the known-good version. 3. DoctorCommand reports installed vs pinned version and runs a dry invocation. 4. Version-mismatch surfaces as typed error with clear remediation, not a cryptic module error.
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: vault-scan false positives: Google Fonts URLs
description:
The vault-scan tool flags Google Fonts CDN URLs as 'Basic Auth URL' findings due to the @ symbol in font parameter strings.

**Affected files:** Design reference HTML files in Prevention Coach project

**Example false positive:**
https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Material+Symbols+Outlined:wght,FILL@100..700,0..1&display=swap

The @ in font specs (e.g., wght,FILL@100..700) triggers the Basic Auth URL pattern.

**Recommended fixes:**
1. Add exclusion pattern for fonts.googleapis.com URLs in vault-scan
2. Or improve Basic Auth URL detection to require actual username:password format before @

**Files affected:** 6 HTML files in design references (not production code)
close_reason: Fixed: tightened Basic Auth URL regex in vault-scan from '://[^[:space:]]+:[^[:space:]]+@' to '://[^@/:[:space:]]+:[^@/:[:space:]]+@[^/:[:space:]]' — requires actual user:pass@host format, no longer matches query params with @ like Google Fonts URLs

title: Ponytail dedup: collapse identical ActionResult + jsEscape + minor yagni cuts
description: From /ponytail-audit (whole-repo). ~90 lines of over-engineering cuts, no behavior change: (1) 6 byte-identical *ActionResult structs → one BridgeActionResult; (2) jsEscape ×4 + jsEscapeOptional ×2 → one shared helper; (3) camelToSnakeCase one-caller inline; (4) RuleAction.label dead field delete; (5) stripAIResponseJSON fence-strip via split(); (6) SemanticSearchResult relocate to use-site. title: Extract ContactResolutionOptions OptionGroup for --no-contacts/--contacts
description: From /simplify review of pippin-1jm (3 of 4 reviewers converged). The --no-contacts flag is declared as a bare @Flag in 6 command structs (MailCommand list/show/search ×3, MailActivityCommand, MessagesCommand list/search/show ×3), and pippin-1jm added a matching bare --contacts flag to each — now 12 flag declarations duplicated. The repo already uses the @OptionGroup convention for cross-cutting flags (OutputOptions, PaginationOptions). Extract a 'ContactResolutionOptions: ParsableArguments' group holding both @Flags plus a 'shouldResolve(config:) -> Bool' method that internalizes the precedence, then each command declares one @OptionGroup. This also gives AIProviderFactory.shouldResolveContacts a more honest home (it is contacts logic, not AI-provider logic — landed there only because AIProviderFactory owns loadConfig/PippinConfig). Friction: per-command help text varies slightly ('the sender' / 'senders' / 'participant handles' / 'sender handles') — a single generic string ('Don't resolve handles to Apple Contacts names') is an acceptable trade. Out of scope for pippin-1jm because it refactors pre-existing --no-contacts wiring across 6 structs, well beyond that diff.

description: Calendar is the last bridge without timeout bounding. listEvents()/findConflicts() (pippin/CalendarBridge/CalendarBridge.swift:104-117) call store.events(matching:) unbounded and return bare [CalendarEvent] — no Outcome<T>, no timedOut signal. Mail/Notes/Contacts/Reminders all have the pattern. Mirror RemindersBridge.fetchRemindersSync: 15s DispatchSemaphore cap -> Outcome<[CalendarEvent]> -> OutputOptions.emit(..., timedOut:). title: Contacts resolution: config default + morning-briefing N+1 enumerations + doc the last-10 phone heuristic
description: Follow-ups from pippin-2nv /simplify. (1) morning-briefing calls 'mail list' per account (5) + 'messages list', each now building a full CNContactStore index = N+1 address-book enumerations per run. Add a config default (e.g. MessagesConfig/MailConfig resolveContacts: Bool, AIProviderFactory) so the briefing/agents can disable globally without passing --no-contacts everywhere, or cache the index briefly. Best-effort+soft-timeout-bounded so it won't fail, just wasteful. (2) Document the last-10-digits phone-matching heuristic in user-facing docs (docs/) — a cross-country-code collision (two numbers sharing last 10 digits) resolves first-write-wins and is shown as a confident name with no fuzzy-match signal; rare but worth documenting. title: messages search: surface attributedBody scan-cap truncation + Q2 early-exit
description: Follow-ups from pippin-cc1 /simplify review. (1) CORRECTNESS-VISIBILITY: searchMessages decode-scans only the most-recent searchAttributedScanCap=1500 attributedBody-only messages; an attributedBody-only message older than that window is silently unsearchable (text-column matches remain unbounded). Surface this to the user like excludedCount — e.g. a scanTruncated/scannedCap hint in the agent envelope + a note in docs/mcp-server.md — so 'no match' isn't mistaken for 'definitely absent'. (2) PERF: Q2 always decodes up to 1500 blobs even when Q1 already satisfied limit with newer matches; a recency-based early-exit (skip Q2 when Q1 has >=limit matches all newer than MAX(date) of blob-only rows) avoids wasted NSUnarchiver work. Bounded today by the cap, so optimization not blocker. (3) MINOR: Q1 selects attributedBody it never decodes (shared base SELECT); listConversations runs two correlated subqueries (last_text + last_attributedbody) over the same latest row — could combine. All bounded by the outer LIMIT.
close_reason: Closed

description: Follow-up from pippin-yux /simplify altitude review. Lifting --fields into OutputOptions means every command with OutputOptions now ACCEPTS --fields, but only mail/calendar/contacts/notes/reminders list+search wire it. Sites like 'mail accounts', 'mail mailboxes', 'reminders lists' return arrays of objects via printAgent(x) and could trivially honor --fields (printAgent already takes a fields: param) — cheap win that shrinks the surprise surface. Other sites (status, doctor, create/mutation result emitters) legitimately can't project meaningfully. Decide per-site: wire the trivial array sites, and/or document the accept-but-ignore set. Low priority, no correctness impact (flag is silently ignored, not errored).
