title: Homebrew-built pippin is ad-hoc signed (brew sandbox has no keychain access)
description: The formula calls scripts/sign.sh, but Homebrew's build sandbox can't reach the login keychain, so security find-identity returns nothing and sign.sh hits its ad-hoc fallback. Result: brew-installed pippin is ad-hoc → TCC grants won't persist for it. Mitigation today: ~/.local/bin/pippin (make install, signed) shadows brew on PATH, so the active/MCP binary IS signed. Options to explore: post-install re-sign hint, or document make install as the signed path. Low priority — active binary is correct.
close_reason: Fixed: formula installs the pre-signed release tarball (url+sha256) for tagged versions instead of building from source — brew binary is now Developer ID signed (verified: codesign Authority=Developer ID, doctor [ok], formula test asserts it). --HEAD still source-builds. Release skill + CLAUDE.md + gotcha doc updated.

title: Full status check times out; works with narrow queries
description:
Observed (Matthew's note): Pippin (Apple Mail/Calendar) — timed out on full status check. That's a known thing when the request is too broad — it works fine with narrow queries.

Reproduction surface: `pippin status` (StatusCommand). The MCP-exposed full-system dashboard.

Suspected root cause (from a quick code read, NOT a fresh trace):

`pippin/Commands/StatusCommand.swift:buildStatusReport` runs six gather functions sequentially with no overall deadline and no parallelism:

  gatherMailStatus → gatherCalendarStatus → gatherRemindersStatus → gatherMemosStatus → gatherNotesStatus → gatherContactsStatus → gatherPermissions

Hot spot is `gatherMailStatus` (StatusCommand.swift:116-127): calls `MailBridge.listAccounts()` then for **each** account `MailBridge.listMailboxes(account:)` — N+1 osascript subprocesses, no per-call timeout passed in, relying on whatever MailBridge picks. With the 5-account setup documented in TIMEOUT_ANALYSIS.md, that's 6 sequential osascript spawns before we've touched Calendar/Notes/Contacts. Notes gather has its own caveat already (countNotes was introduced precisely because listNotes(500) hit the 35s ScriptRunner cap and "dragged status to ~84s" — see comment at StatusCommand.swift:210-212). gatherPermissions also re-probes Mail.

Under the MCP runChild 60s cap (see TIMEOUT_ANALYSIS.md §5 / MailBridge.clampHardTimeout), sequential whole-system gather can blow past the cap on a real account setup, even though each individual `pippin mail accounts`, `pippin calendar events --today`, etc. finish in <22s when called directly. That matches Matthew's "broad query times out, narrow queries fine."

Why this is low-medium priority: there's a known-good workaround (call the narrow commands), the bridges themselves are now well-behaved post-pippin-097/56k/7h4, and the bigger Mail timeouts were already fixed (see TIMEOUT_ANALYSIS.md). What's missing is StatusCommand-level orchestration.

Suggested fixes (pick one or stack):
- Run the six gathers concurrently in a TaskGroup; cap total wall time with a withTimeout wrapper (pippin-91n already proposes such a wrapper for EventKit bridges — reuse it here).
- Give each section its own deadline and emit a partial-results StatusReport when a section misses it (mirror the soft-timeout pattern MailBridge already uses).
- Skip Mail-mailbox enumeration in status (count accounts only) — full mailbox enumeration is a status-page luxury.
- Add `--scope mail,calendar,...` to make narrowness first-class instead of a workaround.

Suspected files:
- pippin/Commands/StatusCommand.swift  (buildStatusReport, gatherMailStatus)
- pippin/MailBridge/MailBridge.swift   (per-call timeout knobs already exist)

I did NOT pull fresh logs or repro this run — the observation is Matthew's; the analysis above is from reading the code. Worth confirming the timeout actually originated in StatusCommand vs. an MCP-layer cap before fixing.

Related: pippin-91n (withTimeout wrapper for EventKit bridges) and the pippin-097/56k/7h4 detachBlocking cleanups.
close_reason: Fixed in e9e028e: status gathers run concurrently under a wall-clock budget (BatchBudget: 50s MCP / unbounded CLI); partial-results timedOut flag instead of SIGKILL. New ConcurrentSlot + runConcurrentlyWithBudget primitive (5 deterministic tests). Verified ~7s on 6-account/511-note setup; 1835 tests green.

title: access_denied remediation block is Voice-Memos-specific, wrong for Calendar/Contacts/Reminders denials
description: WHY: RemediationCatalog.forCode("access_denied") returns a single entry whose human_hint and doctor_check are Voice-Memos/Full-Disk-Access-specific ('Voice Memos requires Full Disk Access... doctor_check: Voice Memos access'). But access_denied is shared by Calendar, Contacts, Reminders, and Notes denials too — those need TCC grants for their respective privacy categories (Calendars/Contacts/Reminders), NOT Full Disk Access. WHAT: observed during pippin-3gp E2E: 'pippin calendar create --start "2027-01-15 14:30" --format agent' on a TCC-denied host returns error.message correctly ('Calendar access denied. -> Open System Settings > Privacy & Security > Calendars...') but the structured error.remediation block underneath says 'Voice Memos requires Full Disk Access...' with doctor_check 'Voice Memos access' — contradictory and misleading for an agent that reads the structured remediation. FIX: either make the remediation bridge-aware (the LocalizedError already carries the correct top-line text per bridge — surface that), or drop/Generalize the access_denied catalog remediation so it doesn't assert Voice-Memos-specific guidance for non-Memos denials. Check RemediationCatalog + AgentError.from wiring. Low priority / cosmetic but agent-facing.
close_reason: Fixed by RemediableError protocol (commits 8748dc7/7709ffc): Calendar/Contacts/Reminders bridge errors now return per-permission .privacyAccess remediation; AgentError.from resolves typed remediation first via RemediationCatalog.resolve(for:). Verified in main.

title: ArgumentParser validation errors exit 5 (tool/bridge) in agent mode, should be 2 (usage)
description: WHY: After pippin-kzi, agent-mode ArgumentParser errors (ValidationError/CommandError) now surface the correct error.message, but PippinExitCode.from() has no mapping for code 'command_error'/'validation_error', so they fall through to the default exit 5 (tool/bridge failure). A malformed flag is a usage/bad-input error and should map to exit 2 (matching the documented typed-exit-code contract: 2=usage). An agent branching on exit class to distinguish 'I sent bad input' (retry with fixed args) from 'the tool/bridge broke' (don't retry) currently gets the wrong signal for validation failures. WHAT: 'pippin calendar create --start bad --format agent' returns error.message correctly but exits 5; should exit 2. FIX: in PippinExitCode.from (pippin/Formatting/PippinExitCode.swift), map ArgumentParser-originated error codes (command_error/validation_error, or detect via String(reflecting:) ArgumentParser. prefix) to exit code 2. Note ArgumentParser's own non-agent path already uses 64 for parse errors; keep that for true parse failures vs in-run ValidationError if they differ. Verify against docs/mcp-server.md exit-code table. Found while fixing pippin-kzi.
close_reason: Closed

title: AudioBridge: carry STT contract (generate/legacy) as an enum on STTEntry instead of re-deriving via string match
description: WHY: sttEntryIsGenerate(_:) classifies the mlx-audio contract by string-matching executable.lastPathComponent / prefixArgs, and is called at two sites (transcribe, buildSTTArgs). resolveSTTEntry() already knows the contract at discovery time. Surfaced by /simplify altitude review of pippin-8ik. WHAT: add an STTContract enum {generate, legacy} field to STTEntry, set it once in resolveSTTEntry, and read entry.contract at call sites — removes the string-match smell and is compile-safe when a third contract variant is added. Acceptable as-is for now (2 contracts); do before the next mlx-audio entry-point change.
acceptance_criteria: STTEntry carries its contract; buildSTTArgs/transcribe branch on the stored enum; no executable/prefixArgs string-matching outside resolveSTTEntry.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract a shared withTemporaryFile helper (AudioBridge, BrowserBridge, AudioConverter duplicate the UUID+defer temp-file pattern)
description: WHY: transcribeViaGenerate (AudioBridge), runNodeScript (BrowserBridge ~line 371), and AudioConverter all hand-roll FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory.appendingPathComponent("pippin-...-\(UUID())") + defer cleanup. Surfaced by /simplify reuse review of pippin-8ik. WHAT: extract a shared 'withTemporaryFile(prefix:) { url in ... }' utility so cleanup semantics live in one place. Out of scope for the pippin-8ik fix (spans 3 bridges).
acceptance_criteria: A single temp-file helper exists and the three bridges use it instead of duplicating the UUID+defer pattern.
close_reason: Added shared withTemporaryFile/temporaryFileURL helper (pippin/TemporaryFile.swift, 6 tests); AudioBridge.transcribeViaGenerate, BrowserBridge.runNodeScript, and AudioConverter now use it instead of hand-rolling temporaryDirectory+UUID+defer.

title: calendar create: accept space-separated 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM' datetime (ergonomics for agents)
description: WHY: calendar create --start requires 'YYYY-MM-DD' or strict ISO-8601 (T-separated). Agents and humans naturally emit 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM' (space-separated), which is rejected — and via MCP the rejection is opaque (see the ValidationError-swallowing bug). 'calendar smart-create' handles NL but is a separate, heavier AI path. WHAT: low-risk ergonomic win — accept a space between date and time by normalizing 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM[:SS]' to ISO before parsing. Keep strict ISO as canonical. MINOR / nice-to-have surfaced during 44-tool MCP E2E.
acceptance_criteria: calendar create --start '2026-06-04 12:00' succeeds and creates the event at that local time; existing ISO/date-only inputs still work.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extend mail body cache to search --body path
description: Follow-up to pippin-8fq (which shipped the 2-pass metadata+batch-body-fetch cache for mail list --preview). Out of that PR. The body match lives inside the JXA loop interleaved with date-range filter, subject/sender match, messageId() dedup (MailBridgeScripts.swift:276), --to filter, offset-skip and per-mailbox/global limit short-circuits. Moving it to Swift forces re-implementing dedup + offset + limit + newest-first ordering across two passes with byte-identical output, and changes the single-window timeout semantics + verbose telemetry (messagesExamined). Higher risk, less repeat-friendly payoff (query-dependent match set). Revisit once the batch script + assembler bake in production; it can reuse buildBatchBodiesScript with a search-specific assembler.
notes: Re-evaluated 2026-06-08 during backlog sweep (alongside pippin-u39/pippin-yux). Precondition still unmet: the --body match remains entirely inside the JXA loop (MailBridgeScripts.swift:251-257), interleaved with subject/sender match + messageId dedup + offset-skip + per-mailbox/global limit short-circuits; no search-specific batch assembler exists yet. Implementing now would require a byte-identical 2-pass Swift rewrite of dedup/offset/limit/newest-first ordering + changed timeout/telemetry semantics — high risk — for a query-dependent (low cache-hit) payoff. Deliberately kept deferred rather than shipped with this release. Revisit when a search-specific buildBatchBodiesScript assembler is warranted by demand.
close_reason: Won't-do (2026-06-12 verification). Fresh code read confirms both prior deferral analyses: searchMessages (MailBridge.swift:120) bypasses MailBodyCache; no search-specific assembler exists (assemblePreviews is preview-only); search discards bodies (body:null, MailBridgeScripts.swift:297). No low-risk slice: (1) write-through impossible — search fetches msg.content() only to test match then returns body:null, so caching it would change result shape + bloat JSON; (2) read-reuse impractical — match is in the JXA loop, the cache is Swift-side, injecting cached bodies into osascript means serializing large bodies into the script (defeats purpose) + staleness; (3) full 2-pass rewrite REGRESSES the common cold-cache path — for --body, pass 1 can't pre-filter (a body-only match would be dropped) so pass 2 must fetch the ENTIRE scan window's bodies, whereas the current inline loop short-circuits at 'limit' matches newest-first (line 238) and fetches far fewer. High-risk byte-identical dedup/offset/limit/ordering rewrite for a query-window-dependent (low cache-hit) payoff. Re-file if real demand for repeated --body searches over the same window emerges.

title: Extend mail body cache to bulk list --preview / search --body path
description:
Follow-up to pippin-e6m (the readMessage body cache, shipped v0.25.0). The body cache only covers the single-message readMessage seam (mail show, mail index, semantic search). The bulk paths — 'mail list --preview N' and 'mail search --body' — issue msg.content() per message INSIDE the JXA/osascript loop (MailBridge/MailBridgeScripts.swift), so they never touch the Swift-side MailBodyCache and still pay the full per-message IMAP download. These are the operations whose cross-account timeouts scale to 95-115s (see CLAUDE.md timeout scaling + TIMEOUT_ANALYSIS.md), so they're where caching would help most.

Restructure needed: split the bulk flow into (1) a fast metadata-only enumeration via JXA (no preview/body), then (2) fill preview/body text per message from MailBodyCache, calling readMessage (cached) only on misses. Tradeoffs to weigh: this does N osascript round-trips on a COLD cache (slower than the single bulk JXA script today), only winning once warm — so likely gate behind a flag or a heuristic (e.g. only when cache hit-rate is high, or only for re-runs). Bodies are immutable per compound id so the cache is valid; read/unread metadata must still come from the live enumeration. Deferred from v0.25.0 and documented in docs/gotchas/jxa.md and the CHANGELOG mail-cache entry.
close_reason: Shipped: mail list --preview now reads/writes through MailBodyCache via 2-pass metadata+batch-body-fetch; warm cache ~11× faster (7s→0.6s); --no-cache escape; shared jsExtractMessageRow fragment keeps cache coherent with mail show

title: Lift --fields into OutputOptions universally (consolidate per-command options)
description: Follow-up to pippin-1k0. --fields is currently a per-command @Option threaded into emit/printAgent at ~9 call sites. The deeper fix is to move --fields into the shared OutputOptions option group so every structured command inherits it uniformly and the per-command declarations + json-mode custom branches collapse. Deferred from the agent-mode fix because rerouting the existing json-mode output paths (which differ in formatting: some compact .sortedKeys, some prettyPrinted) risks output-format churn/regressions and needs careful golden-output testing. Also: extend --fields to mail search/activity, and standardize Calendar on the emit() routing (it currently calls printAgent directly while Notes/Reminders/Mail use emit).
close_reason: Lifted --fields into OutputOptions (universal flag); removed per-command declarations across Calendar/Notes/Reminders/Mail/Contacts sourcing from output.fields; added --fields to mail search, mail activity, calendar search. Zero JSON-formatting changes (avoided the flagged regression surface). 3 new parse tests; 1843 green.

title: Surface Reminders 15s fetch-timeout into the agent envelope (timedOut warning)
description: RemindersBridge.fetchRemindersSync already bounds the EventKit fetch at 15s and returns partial results, but the timeout signal is discarded (only a stderr line). MCP/agent clients get no structured partial-results warning. Lighter alternative to deferred pippin-91n (full async withAsyncTimeout helper, still speculative). Make listReminders/searchReminders return RemindersBridge.Outcome<[ReminderItem]> carrying timedOut, and surface it via the agent envelope warnings (+ JSON/text parity), mirroring ContactsBridge/MailBridge. Calendar uses synchronous events(matching:) with no bounded wait, so it's out of scope.
close_reason: Shipped lighter scope: listReminders/searchReminders return Outcome<[ReminderItem]> with timedOut from the existing 15s fetch cap, surfaced via agent-envelope warnings + JSON/text parity + digest warnings. 1725 tests pass.

title: CI: GitHub Actions Node.js 20 deprecation (checkout, action-gh-release)
description: release.yml run 26805059477 warned: actions/checkout@34e1148 and softprops/action-gh-release@a06a81a run on Node.js 20, deprecated — forced to Node 24 by 2026-06-16, Node 20 removed 2026-09-16. Bump these (and any other Node-based actions in .github/workflows/ + .forgejo/workflows/) to Node-24-compatible versions (re-pin SHAs). Low urgency until June.
close_reason: Bumped checkout v4→v5.0.1, action-gh-release v2.5.0→v3.0.0, cache v4.3.0→v5.0.5 to Node-24 releases (re-pinned SHAs); setup-xcode + codeql-action already Node 24

title: digest --calendar-days upcoming window off-by-one (drops last day)
description: DigestCommand computes upcomingEnd = startOfDay + calendarDays, giving calendarDays-1 full days beyond today (6 for default 7). Help says 'Days of upcoming calendar events beyond today', so an event on the Nth day beyond today is silently missing. Fix: anchor to endOfDay (upcomingEnd = endOfDay + calendarDays).
close_reason: reminders due-before/after now exclude undated reminders (passesDueFilters); digest upcoming window anchored to endOfDay (full N days) + calendar-days bounded 1-366. 5 tests. make ci green (1717 tests). Pushed 0c87069.

title: MCP positional values (search query/id/title) still misparse on leading dash
description: Follow-up to pippin-xbm. notes/reminders/contacts/messages_search pass query as a POSITIONAL (argv.append), and several tools append positional id/title. A positional starting with '-' still trips ArgumentParser. Fix: restructure those buildArgs to append the positional LAST after a '--' separator (options + --format agent before it). Verify empirically that ArgumentParser accepts '[cmd sub --format agent --opts -- -dashvalue]'.
close_reason: ArgHelpers.appendPositionalLast() appends free-form positionals (search query, reminder title) LAST behind a -- separator across 6 buildArgs (mail/reminders/contacts/notes/messages_search + reminders_create). Empirically verified -- form parses. 2 regression tests. make ci green (1704 tests). Pushed 3892d71.

title: Date parse/format misbehave under non-Gregorian device calendar
description: parseDateString (MemosCommand:567, used by memos/summarize --since validate+filter) builds a fixed yyyy-MM-dd DateFormatter without locale=en_US_POSIX → a non-Gregorian device calendar (Thai Buddhist, Japanese era) misparses/rejects the year. TextFormatter.formatDate (compactDate) uses Calendar.current → emits wrong era year (e.g. 2567) in YYYY-MM-DD display strings. Codebase already sets en_US_POSIX in ~10 other formatters; these two are the omissions. Fix: en_US_POSIX on parseDateString; Calendar(identifier:.gregorian) in formatDate (keep local tz).
close_reason: parseDateString sets locale=en_US_POSIX; TextFormatter.formatDate uses Calendar(identifier:.gregorian) (local tz preserved). Fixes non-Gregorian device calendar misparse/misrender. 4 tests (exact UTC epoch, Gregorian-year render). make ci green (1699 tests). Pushed 7c1bf2e.

title: memos export --all silently drops per-item failures in agent/JSON mode
description: Export.run() collects per-memo failures but only prints FAILED to stdout in non-structured mode. In --format agent/json, failed exports vanish — an all-fail batch returns empty results, status ok, exit 0, indistinguishable from 'nothing to export'. batchTimedOut also only went to stderr. Fixed: exportWarnings() pure helper surfaces failures+budget as envelope warnings (agent) / stderr (json/plain), following the digest/timedOut warnings pattern.
close_reason: exportWarnings() helper surfaces per-item failures + batchTimedOut as envelope warnings (agent) / stderr (json/plain); no longer silently dropped. 4 unit tests. make ci green (1696 tests). Pushed 537c6f4.

title: SessionManager persists outside the mutation lock → lost-update race
description: Mutators released the lock then called save(), which re-snapshotted under a separate lock and wrote atomically outside it. Two concurrent mutators' unordered atomic writes could let an older snapshot's write land last, reverting a sibling field on disk (lost update if process exits during concurrent session activity). Fixed: persistLocked() runs inside the mutation's lock so mutate+write is one ordered atomic op.
close_reason: persistLocked() now runs inside the mutation lock so mutate+atomic-write is one ordered op; no out-of-lock snapshot, no lost-update race. Round-trip + 300-way concurrency stress tests added. make ci green (1688 tests). Pushed c5a701e.

title: ActionsCommand: collectMailItems/collectNoteItems blocking JXA not hopped via detachBlocking
description: ActionsCommand.run() (async) calls the sync helpers collectMailItems(since:) and collectNoteItems(since:) directly (run() lines ~85/90). Those helpers call MailBridge.listActivity / NotesBridge.listNotes — blocking osascript subprocesses — on the cooperative thread pool without a detachBlocking hop. This is the same cooperative-thread-stall risk pippin-avc fixed for direct call sites, but the detach-blocking lint cannot catch it because the blocking calls are lexically inside SYNC helper functions (the lint intentionally ignores sync funcs). Fix: wrap the collect* calls at the async boundary in run() with detachBlocking (handle Sendable — the helpers capture self properties account/limit; extract locals or make them static taking params), OR mark the helpers so they run detached. Related: pippin-avc, and the follow-up to extend the lint. Consider whether the lint can flag 'sync func that contains a blocking call AND is called from async without detach' (harder interprocedural analysis).
close_reason: Fixed: made collectMailItems/collectNoteItems static (taking account/limit params instead of self), and wrapped both calls in ActionsCommand.run() with detachBlocking so the blocking MailBridge.listActivity / NotesBridge.listNotes osascript subprocesses hop off the cooperative pool. Build + 1669 tests green; detach lint clean.

title: Extend detach-blocking lint to non-Mail bridges (Notes/Reminders/Calendar/Contacts/Memos)
description: scripts/lint-detach-blocking.py currently flags only MailBridge.(read|list|search)*, MCPServerRuntime.runChild, sendSynchronousRequest, and DispatchSemaphore. NotesBridge/RemindersBridge/CalendarBridge/ContactsBridge/MemosBridge also shell out to blocking JXA/EventKit/GRDB work and have the same cooperative-thread-stall risk under mcp-server fan-out. Broaden BLOCKING_PATTERNS to cover their blocking entry points (e.g. NotesBridge.search/list, ContactsBridge.*, etc.), audit for unwrapped async call sites the way pippin-avc did for Mail, and wrap any gaps in detachBlocking. Keep the async-aware/sync-func-ignore logic.
close_reason: Extended scripts/lint-detach-blocking.py to NotesBridge (JXA) and ContactsBridge (sync CNContactStore) entry points, with documented scope: VoiceMemosDB/GRDB (fast local SQLite) and EventKit Reminders/Calendar (async methods; internal wait tracked by pippin-91n) are intentionally excluded. Added 4 self-test fixtures. The extension surfaced 6 real cooperative-pool-blocking gaps in async commands — all fixed by wrapping in detachBlocking: ContactsCommand (listContacts, createContact, searchByEmail, searchByName, listGroups) and DigestCommand notes section (listNotes). Also added conditional 'extension BridgeOutcome: Sendable where T: Sendable' (public type needs explicit conformance to cross the detachBlocking boundary). NotesCommand correctly NOT flagged — it's a sync command (run() throws, not async). Build + 1672 tests green; lint + self-test clean.

title: Notes pagination: push offset into JXA list script for true deep pagination
description: Follow-up to pippin-6my. NotesBridge.listNotes has no native offset, so NotesCommand pagination over-fetches offset+pageSize+1 and slices in-memory, capped by NotesBridge.maxListLimit (500). The interim fix throws a clear error past the ceiling rather than silently truncating, but deep pagination beyond 500 notes is unsupported. Proper fix: add offset support to buildListScript (skip the first N notes in JXA, return only pageSize) so each page fetches a bounded window from any offset. Watch the soft-timeout interaction: JXA still iterates offset+pageSize notes, so very deep offsets on huge vaults may hit the 22s soft cap and return partial — surface timedOut honestly. Consider whether Apple Notes JXA exposes an efficient indexed slice to avoid O(offset) enumeration per page.
close_reason: Added native offset pushdown to Notes list pagination. buildListScript + listNotes gained an offset param; the JXA results loop now starts at offset (skips the first N sorted notes, returns only the limit window). NotesCommand.runPaginated switched from over-fetch(offset+pageSize+1)+in-memory-slice to native fetch(pageSize+1 @ offset) + Pagination.pageFromPushdown — mirroring MailCommand's established pushdown pattern. This lifts the old 500-note pagination ceiling (removed the 'Cannot paginate beyond 500' throw): body/plaintext fetches stay bounded to the page window, while the all-notes sort enumeration is bounded by the 22s soft cap (surfaced honestly as timedOut), not a fixed offset ceiling. Updated maxListLimit doc. Added 3 offset regression tests; full suite 1672 green, lint clean. NOTE: sorted pagination is inherently O(n) enumeration per page (must sort all to order); Apple Notes JXA has no efficient indexed slice, and the soft cap is the guard.

title: Drift: dependency pins consistent only at current state, no declared upgrade target
description:
Package dependency version policy is internally consistent but not anchored to a repo-declared target:
- Package.swift:8 — allows swift-argument-parser from 1.7.0 and GRDB.swift from 7.10.0 (open-ended 'from:')
- Package.resolved:8 — locks GRDB.swift exactly 7.10.0
- Package.resolved:17 — locks swift-argument-parser exactly 1.7.0

No repo-local target version exists for upgrading either dep. Leave swift-argument-parser 1.7.0 and GRDB.swift 7.10.0 UNCHANGED until a separate upgrade target is declared — any bump beyond those is a suggestion, not a repo-backed alignment. This bead is a placeholder to (optionally) declare an explicit upgrade cadence/target or hash-pin per the supply-chain-hardening policy; low priority.
close_reason: Issue premise was partly outdated: both deps use .upToNextMinor(from:) (NOT open-ended from:), which already IS a declared conservative upgrade target — auto-accept patch releases within the current minor (argument-parser <1.8.0, GRDB <7.11.0), explicit PR required for a new minor. Package.resolved already records exact resolved version + git revision SHA for both (supply-chain hash-pin satisfied). Made the policy explicit with a comment in Package.swift's dependencies block documenting the held floors and that Package.resolved is the authoritative pin. Versions left UNCHANGED (1.7.0 / 7.10.0) per the issue's instruction. Manifest verified to parse.

title: do command: MCPServerRuntime.runChild in serial loop runs on cooperative pool (missing detachBlocking)
description: DoCommand.run (pippin/Commands/DoCommand.swift, runChild call ~line 93) invokes MCPServerRuntime.runChild (blocking process.waitUntilExit) in a serial for-loop on the async cooperative thread with no detachBlocking hop. Lower severity than BatchCommand (one sequential wait at a time, not N parked lanes) but still parks a cooperative thread per step under mcp-server. Fix: wrap each runChild call in 'await detachBlocking { ... }'.
close_reason: Wrapped runChild in detachBlocking in the do-step loop; build + tests green

title: Probe subprocesses read pipe after waitUntilExit() with no concurrent drain (deadlock if >64KB)
description: tryGetSecret (AIProvider/AIProviderFactory.swift:116-127) calls process.waitUntilExit() before readDataToEndOfFile() with no concurrent drain and no timeout. Same shape in DoctorCommand python probes (~470-492) and AudioBridge version/import probes (~66,83,317,334). If a child emits >64KB the pipe buffer fills, child blocks on write, never exits, wait hangs forever. Low impact today (probes emit tiny output: secret value, version string, exit code). Fix: drain on a background queue before waitUntilExit() (the established runProcess pattern) or add a timeout.
close_reason: Fixed the real deadlock in tryGetSecret (AIProviderFactory): stderr was an undrained Pipe() — a chatty get-secret (>64KB stderr) would block on a full buffer and hang waitUntilExit() forever. Now uses nullDevice stderr + concurrent stdout drain + 10s SIGTERM/SIGKILL timeout. The other probe sites (AudioBridge detectVersion/resolvePython3OnPath) use nullDevice stderr + emit tiny stdout (version string / path), so the >64KB deadlock cannot trigger there — verified safe-in-practice.

title: Add async withTimeout wrapper for EventKit bridges (Reminders, Calendar)
description: Reminders + Calendar bridges are EventKit-backed and async — hang risk is low in practice (EventKit predicates are efficient). Deferred from the v0.20.1/v0.20.2 MCP-latency pass. Revisit IF a real hang is reported against reminders_list, calendar_events, or findConflicts on a large vault/wide date range. Then: add a generic withAsyncTimeout helper (Swift Task.timeout / TaskGroup race pattern), thread softTimeoutMs through, and reuse OutputOptions.emit(...,timedOut:,...) for the warning envelope. Captured so the skipped scope isn't lost.
notes: Re-verified 2026-06-03: lighter alternative shipped as pippin-w3y — surfaces the existing 15s fetchRemindersSync cap into the agent envelope as a timedOut warning (no speculative async helper, no Sendable fight). The full generic withAsyncTimeout helper remains deferred/speculative; revisit only if a real hang is reported. Calendar still has no bounded wait (synchronous events(matching:)), so it's out of scope here too.

title: Lift Outcome<T> into a shared bridge module
description: Outcome<T> (results + timedOut flag, back-compat decoder) lives in pippin/NotesBridge/NotesBridge.swift. Once ContactsBridge gains its own copy (pippin-hlw), consider extracting to pippin/Formatting/BridgeOutcome.swift (or similar shared location) so future bridges reuse a single definition. Non-urgent; duplication is 30 lines per bridge, and premature consolidation risks coupling unrelated bridges. Revisit if a third bridge picks up the pattern.
notes: Partial progress in v0.20.2: shared SoftTimeout enum now centralizes defaultMs + clamp, which covered an adjacent duplication found during the simplify pass. The Outcome<T> struct itself remains duplicated (NotesBridge.Outcome has a custom Decodable init for JXA JSON; ContactsBridge.Outcome is pure Swift no-Decodable). Lifting those into a shared module still requires resolving that shape mismatch — e.g. split into OutcomeCore<T> + DecodableOutcome<T>, or just leave them separate. Revisit when a 3rd bridge adopts the pattern.
close_reason: Lifted the duplicated Outcome<T> (results + timedOut + JXA back-compat decoder) into a shared pippin/Formatting/BridgeOutcome.swift. NotesBridge.Outcome and ContactsBridge.Outcome are now typealiases to BridgeOutcome<T>. Resolved the Notes(Decodable)/Contacts(pure-Swift) shape mismatch the issue flagged via Swift CONDITIONAL CONFORMANCE — extension BridgeOutcome: Decodable where T: Decodable — so Notes gets the JXA-JSON decoder and Contacts never exercises it (no coupling). MailBridge.ScanOutcome left separate by design (non-generic, 'messages'-named). Two divergent struct defs → one. Build + 1669 tests green (incl. Notes back-compat decode + Contacts construction tests).

title: pippin doctor: add mail_list/mail_activity/mail_search latency probes
description:
Bug report 2026-04-23 suggested step #1 was "Run `pippin doctor` and check mail-specific permissions and index status" — today `pippin doctor` does not probe mail-bridge latency or surface whether Mail.app is ready. After pippin-kis + pippin-tl8 land, add per-tool timing probes so future hangs are diagnosable without reading source.

Probes to add:
- Mail.app ready poll round-trip time (jsMailReadyPoll latency)
- `mail list --limit 1` cold-start time
- `mail activity --since <now-1h> --limit 1` cold-start time  
- `mail search "" --limit 1` cold-start time (uses new soft-timeout path)

Flag as yellow if any probe exceeds 20s, red if exceeds 55s (would fail MCP clients).
notes: Depends on pippin-kis and pippin-tl8 landing so the probes don't themselves hang. Related: pippin-5et (AIProvider MCP-friendly timeout).
close_reason: Completed the doctor --latency probe set. list/activity/search probes + classifyLatency thresholds (20s warn/.skip, 55s fail) were already present; added the missing 4th probe from the spec: 'Mail.app ready latency' via new MailBridge.probeReady() (runs jsMailReadyPoll only, isolating Mail.app launch/sync time from per-query latency). Added as the first probe; updated --latency help (ready/list/activity/search, ~70s). runMailLatencyProbes() is invoked inside detachBlocking (DoctorCommand.run), so probeReady's bridge call is properly hopped. Build + 1669 tests green; detach lint clean.

title: Surface Notes Outcome.timedOut in ActionsCommand/DigestCommand/StatusCommand
description: ActionsCommand.collectNoteItems, DigestCommand notes section, and StatusCommand.gatherNotesStatus all call NotesBridge.listNotes()/listFolders() and discard the new Outcome.timedOut flag via .results. Result: morning digest could silently include partial Notes data with no advisory; status counts could under-report. Surface the flag: digest should add a 'notes' entry to its top-level warnings array; actions should propagate it on its envelope; status should include a 'notes_timed_out' boolean in StatusReport.NotesStatus. Out of scope of the original Notes timeout PR (file pippin-u2z/o5w/22p) because it requires touching unrelated payload schemas.
close_reason: Verified already implemented across all three sites (stale-open): DigestCommand appends a 'notes: scan timed out' entry to the top-level warnings array (DigestCommand.swift:134-135); ActionsCommand aggregates collectMailItems/collectNoteItems timedOut (run() lines 87/92) and emits it on the envelope via emitActions/emitResults(timedOut:); StatusCommand.gatherNotesStatus sets StatusReport.NotesStatus.timedOut = foldersOutcome.timedOut || countResult == nil (StatusCommand.swift:217). Notes Outcome.timedOut is no longer discarded anywhere.

title: AIProvider URLSession timeout: add MCP-friendly variant for Ollama/Claude
description: OllamaProvider/ClaudeProvider use a hardcoded 120s URLSession timeout. That's correct for CLI use (you want to wait for a slow local model) but exceeds the 60s runChild MCP cap, so MCP clients see 'pippin child exceeded 60s and was terminated' instead of 'AI request timed out / model unreachable'. Options: (a) lower for MCP-invoked path only via a mode signal, (b) add a fast preflight ping to Ollama (HEAD /api/tags) before the actual completion call to fail fast when Ollama is down, (c) document the trade-off and accept it. Out of scope for the Notes timeout PR.
close_reason: Already implemented (stale-open). aiRequestTimeoutSeconds() (AIProvider.swift:41-42) returns 50s under MCP (isMCPContext/PIPPIN_MCP=1), 120s in CLI — below the 60s runChild cap so MCP clients get a clean .timeout instead of a SIGKILL. Both ClaudeProvider (26-36) and OllamaProvider (23-38) thread it into URLRequest.timeoutInterval + sendSynchronousRequest(waitTimeoutSeconds: timeout+5). OllamaProvider also implements option (b): a 2s GET /api/version preflight() (lines 58-79) that fails fast with .providerUnreachable + actionable 'ollama serve' hint when the server is down. Both options (a) MCP-mode timeout and (b) fast preflight are present.

title: Voice Memos batch ops (export/list --transcribe): add wall-clock soft cap
description: memos export --all --transcribe and memos list --transcribe --all chew through up to 10000 memos with no per-batch wall-clock cap. Will trigger the 60s MCP runChild hard cap mid-batch with no partial-result return. Needs a soft cap inside BatchCommand parallel job scheduling that returns whatever was completed + a warning advising to narrow the query (e.g. --since, smaller --limit). Out of scope for the Notes timeout PR — file separately because the fix touches parallel job aggregation.
close_reason: Already implemented (stale-open). MemosCommand both batch paths use BatchBudget.forCurrentContext() (50s wall-clock cap under MCP, unlimited CLI): export --all (lines 168-226) and transcribe --all (318-420) iterate chunks, check budget.exceeded per chunk, set batchTimedOut + break, and emit a partial-results warning advising to narrow (smaller --jobs / re-run for remainder / per-memo). Covered by BatchBudgetTests.swift. Returns whatever completed instead of mid-batch SIGKILL at the 60s runChild cap — exactly the issue's ask.

title: Notes folders: add JXA soft timeout (mail_search analogue)
description: NotesBridge.listFolders enumerates every folder, calls .notes().length per folder. Nested unbounded enumeration with no time check. Add 22s soft timeout at the top of the folders loop. Wrap response in {results, meta:{timedOut}}.
close_reason: Shipped in dd7b487: NotesBridge searchNotes/listNotes/listFolders return Outcome<T> with 22s soft timeout in JXA loop; surfaced via shared OutputOptions.emit() helper to stderr/agent/text. Mail-search analogue applied across all three Notes JXA loops.

title: Fix XCTest unavailable in CLT SDK on macOS 26 (swift test fails)
description:
Running 'swift test' (via 'make test') fails with 'no such module XCTest' in AIProviderTests.swift when using the Command Line Tools SDK on macOS 26 (macosx26.4). XCTest.framework is not present in /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk on macOS 26.

Workaround: run with DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer swift test.

Fix: Update Makefile to use xcrun --sdk macosx swift test, or add XCTest search path to Package.swift, or add a note to CLAUDE.md.

The CI workflow (runs on macos-15) is unaffected.
close_reason: Makefile test target now preflights XCTest availability via 'xcrun --sdk macosx --find xctest': (1) found → run normally; (2) missing but Xcode.app installed → transparently re-run under DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer so 'make test' just works on a CLT-only-selected host; (3) neither → print an actionable error (install Xcode / set DEVELOPER_DIR, see pippin-ncr) instead of the cryptic 'no such module XCTest'. Combines with the pre-existing xcrun routing + CLAUDE.md/Makefile docs (the issue's other listed fixes). CI (macos-15, Xcode present) hits the fast path unchanged. Verified: full suite runs green via 'make test'.

title: Fix DispatchSemaphore.wait() blocking cooperative thread in StatusCommand
description:
StatusCommand.run() is async throws. It calls buildStatusReport() → gatherRemindersStatus(), which calls sem.wait(timeout: .now() + .seconds(5)) on the cooperative thread. This blocks a Swift cooperative thread for up to 5s, potentially starving other tasks.

Fix: Wrap the EKEventStore.fetchReminders call with withCheckedContinuation to make gatherRemindersStatus async and deliver the result via continuation.resume().

File: pippin/Commands/StatusCommand.swift ~line 176
Severity: Medium (CLI tool, rarely concurrent)
close_reason: Already resolved: buildStatusReport() (containing the bounded 5s gatherRemindersStatus semaphore) is wrapped in 'await detachBlocking { buildStatusReport() }' at StatusCommand.swift:79 — the wait runs off the cooperative pool and is bounded. Verified, no code change needed.

title: Mail activity scan: combined INBOX + Sent pass for commitment tracking
description:
Cowork feedback 2026-04-20: 'No sent mail scan. I only looked at INBOX. A proper activity scan should also check sent mail for commitments you have made (I will send that by Friday type things). That would need an explicit second call with mailbox: Sent.'

mail_list already accepts a mailbox arg, so the agent can call it twice — the issue is discoverability and efficiency. Two approaches:

1. Tool description nudge: update mail_list description (pippin/MCP/ToolRegistry.swift:173) to explicitly mention agents should also query mailbox=Sent for activity scans. Cheap.

2. New mail_activity / mail_scan tool: single call returns recent messages across INBOX + Sent (and optionally Drafts) merged and sorted, with body previews. Pairs with the mail_list body-preview feature (separate issue). Ideal for morning-briefing / daily-scan workflows.

Prefer (2) since it also fixes the N+1 problem and gives Cowork/morning-briefing a single natural entry point. Depends on the mail body-preview feature.
close_reason: Shipped mail activity subcommand + MCP tool. Combined INBOX+Sent scan with body preview, dedup by messageId, alias resolution with collectAllMailboxes fallback. 25 new tests, all 1149 tests passing. Committed aee4e33+55fda62 (squash-merged in 0d827e0).

title: Unify FDA remediation text between DoctorCommand and RemediationCatalog
description: DoctorCommand.checkVoiceMemosDB() has its own free-form FDA remediation string on DiagnosticCheck; RemediationCatalog has another. They say the same thing but in different words. Refactor so DiagnosticCheck.remediation is backed by a Remediation struct (or pulled from RemediationCatalog by code) — one source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: 1. DiagnosticCheck.remediation is either typed as Remediation? or populated from RemediationCatalog by error code. 2. No duplicated FDA text in the repo (grep 'Full Disk Access' returns one canonical home). 3. Doctor output in text mode reads the same or better than before. 4. Doctor output in JSON mode carries the structured remediation (new key).
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: Type-safe error category enum for RemediationCatalog keys
description: RemediationCatalog.forCode keys on raw strings ('access_denied', etc.) that must match agentErrorCode(for:) output exactly. A typo in either place silently breaks the lookup. Wrap the codes in a closed enum (ErrorCategory: String or similar) and thread it through AgentError + RemediationCatalog + call sites so the compiler enforces the coupling.
acceptance_criteria: 1. New enum ErrorCategory (or similar closed type) replaces string keys in RemediationCatalog. 2. AgentError and RemediationCatalog share the same key type — can't drift. 3. Existing snake_case JSON output unchanged (agent consumers see no schema change). 4. Unit tests for all existing categories still pass.
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: Homebrew postinstall for mlx-audio transcription deps
description: Users installing via brew install pippin currently hit a manual 'pip install mlx-audio' step before memos transcribe works. Extend the Homebrew tap formula to run pipx install mlx-audio==<pinned> as a post_install, and teach AudioBridge to resolve the pipx-installed binary path. Cross-repo work: formula lives in mattwag05/homebrew-tap.
acceptance_criteria: 1. /opt/homebrew/Library/Taps/mattwag05/homebrew-tap/Formula/pippin.rb has a post_install block that runs 'pipx install mlx-audio==<pinned>'. 2. Skip or advise cleanly when pipx is absent. 3. AudioBridge.swift probes for the binary via which + pipx environment, with -m as final fallback. 4. README.md install section documents the new flow. 5. Verified with brew uninstall && brew install mattwag05/tap/pippin on a clean state.
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: AVFoundation audio conversion before transcribe
description: VoiceMemosDB.transcribeMemo() passes source audio paths straight through to the STT backend with no format normalization. When Voice Memos files aren't in a format the backend can handle natively, transcription fails. Add a Swift-native (AVFoundation-based) converter that normalizes to 16 kHz mono WAV before passing to the backend.
acceptance_criteria: 1. New pippin/MemosBridge/AudioConverter.swift implements AVAssetExportSession/AVAudioFile conversion to 16kHz mono WAV. 2. VoiceMemosDB.transcribeMemo() calls converter when source extension isn't in the native set (.wav, .m4a). 3. Converted files land under NSTemporaryDirectory() and are cleaned up after transcription. 4. --keep-converted debug flag preserves the temp file and prints its path.
close_reason: Landed in pippin v0.18.0 (commits 535d021, eb99fd8, 872a64c) and homebrew-tap 33b7883. All six acceptance criteria met; 1098 tests green; doctor smoke verified on M4.

title: Session state persistence
description: Add session state management: remember active account/mailbox context, draft state, operation history with undo capability. Persist to ~/.config/pippin/session.json with file locking. Primarily useful once REPL mode exists — session state carries across commands within a REPL session.

title: Universal --json on all commands
description: Audit and add --format json support to doctor, init, completions, and any other subcommands missing it. Every command should produce deterministic, parseable JSON output when --json is passed. Consistent error format across all commands.
close_reason: Closed

title: Refactor: extract runConcurrently helper shared by TriageEngine and SemanticSearch
description: TriageEngine.runBatchesConcurrently and the concurrent dispatch pattern in SemanticSearch.search() are near-identical (DispatchGroup + NSLock + index-sorted results). Extract a generic runConcurrently<Input, Output> free function in a new ConcurrencyUtils.swift file in MailAIBridge/. SemanticSearch can use it directly; TriageEngine can wrap it for its batch semantics. Eliminates ~12 lines of duplication and unifies error handling.
close_reason: Closed

title: AI provider native structured-output (JSON) enforcement
description:
Split from pippin-cfg (retry half shipped 2026-06-12). Remaining: opt into native structured-output modes where the provider supports them, for the ~7 JSON-expecting complete() callers (IntentPlanner, DataExtractor, ActionExtractor, PromptInjectionScanner, TriageEngine, calendar/reminders smart-create). Deferred from the retry work because it is provider-specific and prompt-coupled — NEEDS per-provider + per-prompt validation, not a blanket flag:

PER-PROVIDER NOTES (gathered during pippin-cfg):
- OpenAI-compat: response_format={"type":"json_object"} REQUIRES the literal word 'json' in the prompt/messages or the API returns HTTP 400. Some endpoints also support {"type":"json_schema", schema}. Blindly enabling could regress calls whose prompts don't contain 'json'. Must verify/inject per prompt.
- Ollama: format:"json" (or format:<schema>) constrains output. BUT the global CLAUDE.md notes Qwen3.6/gemma 'thinking' pollutes/empties JSON unless thinking is disabled (oMLX: chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking=false; M5 Ollama gemma4: reasoning_effort='none'). So format:json must be paired with the thinking-disable knob per model, or it can make JSON output WORSE.
- Claude: NO simple response_format. Native structured output requires forced tool-use (tools + tool_choice) or assistant-message prefill — a larger change. Can be a no-op (fall back to today's prompt-based extraction) since DoCommand's self-repair already mitigates.

DESIGN SKETCH: add an opt-in AICompletionOptions{jsonMode:Bool} via a protocol-extension default so existing 2-arg complete(prompt:system:) callers are unchanged; providers apply response_format/format where safe (guard the OpenAI 'json' requirement; pair Ollama format:json with the thinking-disable knob); Claude jsonMode is best-effort/no-op. Wire only the JSON-expecting call sites, validating each prompt still produces parseable output. LOW priority — self-repair mitigates today (not blocking).
close_reason: Closed

title: Refresh graphify-out knowledge graph (stale since 2026-06-02)
description: graphify-out/ last rebuilt 2026-06-02 (commit 491955c); code has moved through v0.32.0 since (Contacts integration pippin-2nv, Messages attributedBody pippin-cc1, disclaim re-exec pippin-0vr). Run 'graphify update .' (AST refresh, preserves committed community labels in .graphify_labels.json) and commit the regenerated artifacts. Seconds of work; bundle into the next code session.

title: AI provider hardening: retries + structured output enforcement
description: All three providers (Ollama/Claude/OpenAI-compat in pippin/AIProvider/) are single-attempt with stream:false and no structured-output enforcement (no Claude JSON mode, no OpenAI response_format) — plain text extraction + JSON parse. DoCommand's self-repair round-trip mitigates today. Low-priority robustness: add bounded retry on transient network errors and opt into native JSON output modes where the provider supports them. Not blocking any current feature.
close_reason: Retry half shipped (withAIRetry: bounded, budget-aware retry on 429/5xx + network blips across all 3 providers, 7 tests). Structured-output enforcement split to pippin-us2 — it's provider-specific + prompt-coupled (OpenAI response_format needs 'json' in prompt; Ollama format:json needs the thinking-disable knob; Claude needs tool-use) and needs per-prompt validation, too risky to bundle with retry.

title: Decide audio/browser graduation from PIPPIN_EXPERIMENTAL gate
description: Both commands are fully implemented and tested (BrowserCommandTests.swift 604 lines, AudioCommandTests.swift 182 lines) yet gated at pippin-entry/Pippin.swift:23-26 behind PIPPIN_EXPERIMENTAL=1 and absent from the MCP surface. Decide: graduate to default-on, keep gated (document why — Python/mlx-audio runtime dep for audio, headless WebKit footprint for browser), and/or expose as MCP tools behind the same env gate. Product decision, not code-driven.
close_reason: DECISION 2026-06-12: do NOT graduate — confirm removal at v1.0.0 as already announced in the v0.27.0 CHANGELOG deprecation note. Keep gated behind PIPPIN_EXPERIMENTAL=1 for now (existing scripts keep working); code + tests (~783 lines) stay until the v1.0.0 cut removes them. Rationale: both are REDUNDANT with better-maintained dedicated tooling — pippin audio (mlx-audio Python subprocess) duplicates the Kokoro TTS MCP already wired into Talia (speak/list_voices/ping) + Whisper STT on Pironman + Gus edge-tts/faster-whisper; pippin browser (Node + Playwright WebKit) duplicates Claude-in-Chrome MCP + Gus playwright/chromium. Graduating would pull heavy non-Swift deps into the default footprint and add MCP tools that duplicate existing ones, for rarely-used features. The user-facing removal announcement already lives in CHANGELOG (consumed at release time), so no separate tracking issue needed; the v1.0.0 release will action the removal.

title: Prompt-injection rule scanner: substring matching bypassed by whitespace obfuscation
description: PromptInjectionScanner substring patterns (systemPromptOverride/dataExfiltration/roleHijacking) use literal text.range(of:), so 'ignore  previous\ninstructions' (extra spaces/newlines) evades the always-on rule pass. The AI pass (scanWithAI) catches these, but it requires an AI provider + latency. Proposed (behavior-change, needs review): convert these substring patterns to whitespace-tolerant regex (word\s+word) via the existing regexMatches helper, which returns the real matched text so sanitize() redaction still maps to original ranges. Deferred — security-feature behavior change, user should decide.
close_reason: Substring injection patterns (systemPromptOverride/dataExfiltration/roleHijacking) now match via whitespace-tolerant regex (word\s+word) through regexMatches; sanitize() redaction preserved; threat-count semantics preserved (one match per phrase). 5 tests, 18 existing still pass. make ci green (1722 tests). Pushed f432fcd.
